symptom: Azure AD B2C local account passwords expire after 90 days unexpectedly. Users cannot sign in with expired passwords.
rootCause: B2C local accounts created via custom policy or Azure AD Graph API do not have passwordPolicies set to DisablePasswordExpiration by default. Only accounts created by built-in password policy get this automatically. Without this setting, passwords expire after 90 days.
solution: Run PowerShell: Connect-AzureAD → Get-AzureADUser -All $true | Set-AzureADUser -PasswordPolicies DisablePasswordExpiration. Alternatively use Graph API PATCH with {"passwordPolicies":"DisablePasswordExpiration"}. After patching, the original password can be used again immediately. For large tenants, pace writes to under 7000/5min to avoid 429 throttling.
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/=======11. AAD=======/11.10 [B2C] AAD B2C password expiration.md

symptom: MFA Authenticator app displays Object ID instead of UPN when using notification-based verification in Azure China (Mooncake).
rootCause: This is by design (intentional). Azure MFA prevents EUII (end-user identifiable information) from leaving region boundaries, so Object ID is shown instead of UPN in push notifications on both iOS and Android.
solution: Use 'Verification code without notification' mode instead of 'Receive notifications for verification'. This shows UPN correctly. Setup URL for 21V: https://aka.ms/ChinaMFASetup. Note: if Google Play services are unavailable on Android, notification mode won't work anyway — use verification code mode.
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/=======11. AAD=======/11.11 [MFA] MFA with Authenticator app.md

symptom: NPS extension for Azure MFA does not work in Azure China (21Vianet/Mooncake) environment with default configuration.
rootCause: Default NPS extension configuration points to global Azure endpoints. China cloud requires different endpoints for MFA hostname, resource hostname, and STS URL. Additionally, the setup script needs Connect-MgGraph with -Environment China parameter.
solution: 1) Modify AzureMfaNpsExtnConfigSetup.ps1: use Connect-MgGraph -Environment China. Register redirect URIs (https://login.microsoftonline.com/common/oauth2/nativeclient and http://localhost) and set Allow public client flows=Yes in app registration. 2) Set registry HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\AzureMfa: AZURE_MFA_HOSTNAME=strongauthenticationservice.auth.microsoft.cn, AZURE_MFA_RESOURCE_HOSTNAME=adnotifications.windowsazure.cn, STS_URL=https://login.chinacloudapi.cn/. 3) Restart IAS service. 4) Verify service principals exist (981f26a1-7f43-403b-a875-f8b09b8cd720 and 1f5530b3-261a-47a9-b357-ded261e17918).
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/=======11. AAD=======/11.14 [MFA on-prem] AAD MFA with NPS extension.md

symptom: ADFS Azure MFA authentication fails with error after AAD deprecated TLS 1.0/1.1. Schannel/DotNet errors in ADFS event logs when Azure MFA adapter tries to contact Azure MFA service.
rootCause: TLS 1.2 is not enabled on the ADFS server for .NET applications. AAD has deprecated TLS 1.0/1.1, so ADFS MFA adapter cannot establish secure connection to Azure MFA service.
solution: Enable strong authentication for .NET applications by setting registry: New-ItemProperty -path 'HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\.NetFramework\v4.0.30319' -name 'SchUseStrongCrypto' -value '1' -PropertyType 'DWord' -Force. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows-server/identity/ad-fs/operations/manage-ssl-protocols-in-ad-fs#enable-strong-authentication-for-net-applications
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/=======11. AAD=======/11.15 [ADFS] Configure 21V Azure MFA as auth provi.md

solution: Register custom app, grant MS Graph permissions, use cert auth with -Environment China.

symptom: MFA SMS/Voice verification fails with error BlockAllEnterpriseSmsTrafficInCountryExceptAllowedTenants or BlockAllEnterpriseVoiceTrafficInCountryExceptAllowedTenants. Telephony MFA blocked for certain country codes.

symptom: AADSTS500082: SAML assertion is not present in the token — when converting ADFS-AAD federation from WS-fed to SAML protocol.
rootCause: ADFS returns InvalidNameIDPolicy because it cannot provide NameID in the format AAD requests (urn:oasis:names:tc:SAML:2.0:nameid-format:persistent). Also missing IDPEmail attribute in claim rules.
solution: 1) Remove extra RPID, keep only urn:federation:partner.microsoftonline.cn. 2) Change claim rule NameID format to persistent. 3) Add IDPEmail claim rule mapping UPN to IDPEmail. 4) To revert: Set-MsolDomainAuthentication Managed → delete RP → Convert-MsolDomainToFederated → Update-MsolFederatedDomain.

symptom: MFA requests fail due to AAD Gateway throttling — users receive HTTP 429 Too Many Requests during MFA sign-in. MFA service (SAS) never receives the request.
rootCause: AAD Gateway (Layer 1) throttles the request before it reaches SAS. EffectiveStatusCode=429 and IsThrottled=true in RequestSummaryEventCore. This is the highest-impact throttling layer.
solution: 1) Query AAD Gateway AllRequestSummaryEvents: filter EffectiveStatusCode startswith '429', TargetService contains 'mysignins' or 'strongauthenticationservice'. 2) Aggregate by time to identify throttling windows. 3) Gateway throttling blocks all MFA for affected services — escalate to identity platform team if persistent. 4) Kusto cluster (21V): idsharedmcbjb.chinanorth2.kusto.chinacloudapi.cn / AADGatewayProd.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/1. Readiness/----Philip AAD MFA Deep Dive----/2.  Different level of throttling types.md

symptom: MFA SMS/Voice calls not delivered to users in specific countries. SAS logs show BlockAllEnterpriseSms/VoiceTrafficInCountry or RepMap/OptIn keywords.
rootCause: Telephony-level country hard block (RepMap) — Microsoft's fraud protection blocks SMS/Voice MFA delivery to certain countries flagged for telephony fraud. This is not rate-based throttling but a country-level policy.
solution: 1) Search SAS AllSASCommonEvents Msg for 'BlockAll', 'OptIn', 'RepMap' keywords. 2) If country is blocked, customer must use alternative MFA methods (Authenticator app TOTP/Push instead of SMS/Voice). 3) Reference: https://docs.azure.cn/zh-cn/entra/identity/authentication/concept-mfa-telephony-fraud. 4) Kusto cluster (21V): idsharedmcsha.chinaeast2.kusto.chinacloudapi.cn / idmfacne.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/1. Readiness/----Philip AAD MFA Deep Dive----/2.  Different level of throttling types.md

symptom: Azure Files storage account AD DS join script runs successfully but FSLogix/Kerberos authentication fails afterward when accessing Azure Files shares.

symptom: Unable to restore deleted B2C user. Error: 'A conflicting object with one or more of the specified property values is present in the directory' (Request_BadRequest). Browser trace may or may not show which attribute conflicts.
rootCause: The deleted B2C user's AlternativeSecurityId (AltSecID) conflicts with an existing active user. AltSecID is used by external IdP features - type 1=MSA, 5=AAD, 6=BYOI. When a new user is created with the same external identity after the original was deleted, both share the same AltSecID, blocking restore.
solution: 1) Check MSODS Kusto logs (IfxAuditLoggingCommon + IfxUlsEvents) using correlationId from audit log to identify the conflicting attribute. 2) If browser trace shows conflicted attribute name → search existing users for that value. 3) If no attribute shown → check AltSecID: use ASC/Graph API beta to get deleted user's alternativeSecurityIds, then search active users for matching values. 4) Customer must delete or modify the conflicting active user, then restore the deleted user.

symptom: Azure AD B2C local account authentication fails with 'Invalid username or password' even though credentials are correct. Occurs in 21V (Mooncake) when using custom policy with LocalAccounts.
rootCause: Two issues: 1) The ESTS login endpoint in custom policy TrustFrameworkBase was pointing to global (login.microsoftonline.com) instead of 21V endpoint. 2) Issuer validation failed because login.partner.microsoftonline.cn is not recognized by B2C CPIM - must use sts.chinacloudapi.cn/{tenantId}/ as issuer.
solution: In B2C custom policy XML, change the login endpoint to the 21V-specific endpoint: use https://login.chinacloudapi.cn/ or https://sts.chinacloudapi.cn/{tenantId}/ instead of login.partner.microsoftonline.cn. Verify via CPIM Kusto logs (cpimmcprod2.chinanorth2.kusto.chinacloudapi.cn/CPIM) using x-ms-cpim-trans correlation ID.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Account management/B2C/Case Study/2307030040000167 AAD B2C local account authenticat.md

symptom: Azure AD B2C user flow does not return 'emails' claim in token for some users. Users created via Graph API don't get emails claim, but users created via B2C sign-up flow do.
rootCause: Users created via Graph API without proper 'identities' attribute are not recognized as B2C local/consumer accounts. The emails claim comes from the identities attribute (signInName without default domain) and otherMails attribute. Users must have creationType=LocalAccount to be treated as consumer accounts.
solution: When creating B2C users via Graph API, use Example 2 from MS docs (Create user with social and local account identities): include 'identities' array with signInType, issuer, and issuerAssignedId. Also set passwordProfile with forceChangePasswordNextSignIn=false and passwordPolicies. Note: use Graph API beta to view all user attributes (v1.0 omits some). The 'Mail' attribute does NOT contribute to emails claim, only identities signInName and otherMails do.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Account management/B2C/Case Study/2311180030000169 AAD B2C emails claim of user flow.md

symptom: International social IDPs (Google, Facebook, Twitter) cannot be used as B2C identity providers in Mooncake (21V). Users cannot authenticate via these providers.
rootCause: China's national firewall blocks access to Google, Facebook, and Twitter services. These IDPs are still shown in B2C UI but are non-functional in Mooncake environment.
solution: PG is planning to hide blocked IDPs from the UI. Workaround: use Global AAD as IDP for Mooncake B2C - the user OID will be base64-converted into the AlternativeSecurityId attribute. Note: customized login UX might not work properly per B2C docs when using this approach.

symptom: Cross-tenant B2B collaboration fails when customer restricts allowed applications to Office365 only. Guest users cannot login to guest tenant portal even though Office365 app is allowed.
rootCause: MicrosoftAdminPortals app is implicitly required for portal access but not included in the Office365 app bundle. This app cannot be added via Portal UI directly - only via Graph API.
solution: Use Graph API to PATCH crossTenantAccessPolicy partner settings. Add MicrosoftAdminPortals app (AppId: c44b4083-3bb0-49c1-b47d-974e53cbdf3c) and the required portal app (80ccca67-54bd-44ab-8625-4b79c4dc7775) to the b2bCollaborationInbound.applications.targets array alongside Office365. Endpoint: PATCH https://microsoftgraph.chinacloudapi.cn/v1.0/policies/crossTenantAccessPolicy/partners/{guestTenantId}. Prerequisites: register app with Graph permission to modify CrossTenant settings, obtain Graph token.

symptom: AADDS primary DC stops responding after Azure Disk Encryption (ADE) V2 migration maintenance. AADDS auto-healing does not detect this failure type, causing prolonged outage if DNS still points to the failed DC IP.
rootCause: PG maintenance operation migrating encryption-at-rest extension (ADE) from V1 to V2 caused corruption of the primary DC boot disk. AADDS auto-healing does not trigger for this specific failure mode — only PG manual intervention can fix it.
solution: 1) Raise ICM to PG to manually promote secondary DC and recreate failed DC. 2) Update VNet DNS settings to point to the new DC IPs. 3) Restart all domain-joined servers to pick up new DNS. 4) Do NOT put delete locks on AADDS resource group — locks prevent NIC cleanup during DC failover. Reference: https://docs.azure.cn/zh-cn/active-directory-domain-services/network-considerations#network-resources-used-by-azure-ad-ds
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Account management/Entra Domain Service/## Case study/2206180060000017 Unable to connect to AADDS.md

symptom: AADDS DC failover creates new DC with different IP address instead of reusing the original IP. VNet DNS settings become stale, causing domain-joined machines to lose connectivity.
rootCause: Resource group delete lock prevents the old NIC from being deleted when AADDS auto-healing creates a new DC. Since the old NIC still holds the original IP, Azure DHCP assigns a new IP to the replacement DC's NIC. The VNet DNS is not auto-updated.
solution: 1) Remove delete locks from AADDS resource group before DC failover or as a preventive measure. 2) After DC failover, update VNet DNS to new DC IPs. 3) Restart domain-joined servers. 4) If customer needs original IPs back: remove lock, delete old NICs, then PG deletes and recreates DCs in sequence to reclaim IPs. 5) AADDS configuration diagnostics can detect VNet DNS mismatch — run diagnostics periodically.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Account management/Entra Domain Service/## Case study/2206180060000017 Unable to connect to AADDS.md

symptom: AADDS replica DC creation takes 3+ days or fails repeatedly. ARM logs show VM delete operations repeating every hour. Jarvis logs show PSRemoting access denied errors during DC promotion.
rootCause: Networking issue between Azure China regions (e.g., China East 2 and China North 3) causes RemotePowerShell connection drops during DC promotion. AADDS PG confirmed they cannot get root cause as they cannot reproduce in lab. Both successful and failed promotion events show PSRemoting errors, but some succeed after retries.
solution: Short-term: Manually reboot the affected VM during replicaCreate operation to unblock DC promotion. Long-term: PG is developing a remediator to automatically reboot the affected VM. Check ARM Kusto (armmcadx.chinaeast2.kusto.chinacloudapi.cn/armmc) EventServiceEntries for VM delete patterns. Check Jarvis for DC promotion success/failure correlation IDs. Reference ICM: 446491273.

symptom: Azure DevOps pipeline fails with AADSTS700212: No matching federated identity record found for presented assertion audience api://AzureADTokenExchangeChina. Service connection tests also fail.
rootCause: Azure DevOps backend changed the audience from api://AzureADTokenExchange to api://AzureADTokenExchangeChina for Mooncake environment. The federated identity credential on the Managed Identity still has the old audience value. Cloud-specific audiences: Public=api://AzureADTokenExchange, Mooncake=api://AzureADTokenExchangeChina, Fairfax=api://AzureADTokenExchangeUSGov.
solution: Update the audience in the federated identity credential (Azure Portal > Managed Identity > Federated credentials) to match the cloud environment: api://AzureADTokenExchangeChina for Mooncake. Note: DevOps PG may change the audience back, so monitor and adjust accordingly.

symptom: Managed Identity token request returns HTTP 500 Internal Server Error from IMDS endpoint (169.254.169.254). One pod on a VM gets tokens fine while another pod on a different VM fails. Error persists for hours then self-resolves.
rootCause: IMDS node-level issue: PFXImportCertStore failed with 'There is not enough space on the disk' on the hosting node. The MSI certificate store operation fails due to low disk space on the physical node, causing all MSI token requests on that node to return 500.
solution: 1) Check ImdsSpan Kusto table: cluster('azcore.chinanorth3.kusto.chinacloudapi.cn').database('Fa').ImdsSpan with NodeId and statusMessage filters. 2) Check Jarvis ImdsEvent table. 3) If FaultPoint=IdentityException:CertPFXImportCertStoreFailed, this is a node-level issue - file ICM to platform team. 4) Workaround: redeploy workload to different node. Note: ImdsApiReqResp table does NOT work in Mooncake.

symptom: PIM eligible assignment extend request fails with RoleAssignmentRequestPolicyValidationFailed in audit logs. No detailed error message shown in Azure Portal or ASC audit logs.
rootCause: The extend request duration exceeds the PIM policy MaximumGrantPeriod (default 365 days). The ExpirationRule policy validation fails silently with only RoleAssignmentRequestPolicyValidationFailed shown.
solution: 1) Check Jarvis logs with timestamp + correlation ID to see detailed failure reason. 2) Look for 'The duration in the request is greater than maximum allowed duration' in Jarvis logs. 3) Adjust the request StopDateTime to be within MaximumGrantPeriod, or increase MaximumGrantPeriod in PIM role settings. Ref: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/rest/api/authorization/includes/privileged-role-common-errors
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Account management/Privileged Identity Managemnet/Case study/[Case study] PIM - how to check request failure.md

symptom: Customer needs to grant full database access across multiple database types (SQL Database + DBForMySQL) within a specific resource group. No single built-in role covers multiple DB types.
rootCause: Built-in RBAC roles are database-type-specific (e.g. SQL DB Contributor only covers Microsoft.Sql/*). There is no built-in role that covers both Microsoft.Sql/* and Microsoft.DBforMySQL/* simultaneously.
solution: Create a custom RBAC role combining operations: Microsoft.Sql/*, Microsoft.DBforMySQL/*, plus common ops (Authorization/*/read, Insights/alertRules/*, Insights/metrics/read, ResourceHealth/*, Resources/deployments/*, Support/*). Set assignableScopes at subscription level for reuse. Assign at resource group scope.

rootCause: Azure CLI role assignment command first queries AAD Graph to resolve the assignee identity. The SP lacks permission to read AAD user/SP information

rootCause: ADFS reads windowsaccountname from Windows/Kerberos logon session context, preserving case as typed at login. Cached until computer restart

symptom: OAuth ROPC grant fails for federated users against Azure AD. Error code 80047860 in EvoSTS LoginResponse.
rootCause: Azure AD does not forward password to AD FS for ROPC flow. Federated users must have password hash synced. Error 80047860 = federated user auth failure; e5a = managed user wrong password.
solution: Enable Password Hash Sync (PHS) for federated users who need ROPC. For ADFS 2016 direct ROPC: configure Application Group, use grant_type=password with client_id and resource. Post to /adfs/oauth2/token.

symptom: Chrome always prompts certificate selection dialog during ADFS/Azure AD device or client certificate authentication, even with only one cert.
rootCause: Chrome does not auto-select client certificates by default. IE/Edge on Windows 10 auto-selects. iOS/Android Chrome/Safari prompts once and remembers.
solution: Set Chrome policy AutoSelectCertificateForUrls via registry: HKLM\SOFTWARE\Policies\Google\Chrome\AutoSelectCertificateForUrls. Use pattern https://device.login.microsoftonline.com with filter ISSUER CN=MS-Organization-Access for AAD.

rootCause: Get-Credential + -Credential sends creds to ADFS usernamemixed endpoint (basic auth) instead of passive /adfs/ls/ (modern auth browser flow). Blocked by ADFS claim rules.
solution: Use Connect-EXOPSSession without -Credential to trigger browser-based modern auth. If automation needed: add PS client IP exception to basic auth blocking claim rule.

symptom: WAP resets TLS client hello from applications. Application cannot establish SSL connection to ADFS through WAP.
rootCause: Application/WAF does not include SNI extension in TLS Client Hello. Http.sys on WAP filters traffic because it cannot determine which certificate to serve without SNI.
solution: Add fallback certificate to 0.0.0.0:443 on every ADFS and WAP: netsh http show sslcert, then netsh http add sslcert ipport=0.0.0.0:443 certhash=<hash> appid={5d89a20c-beab-4389-9447-324788eb944a}. Long-term: upgrade app for SNI.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/ADFS and WAP/Case Study/[Cert] ADFS with SSL TLS Server Name Indication.md

symptom: ADFS claim rule for x-ms-forwarded-client-ip does not work. MDM managed devices cannot access Exchange Online despite claim rule allowing their IPs.
rootCause: ADFS claim rule parameters (x-ms-*) are case-sensitive. Uppercase letters cause silent failure. x-ms-forwarded-client-ip shows WAP/proxy IP, not actual client IP.
solution: Ensure exact lowercase for claim type URIs. Use regex with word boundaries for IP matching. Collect WAP/ADFS debug+admin+audit logs. Test regex at regex101.com.

symptom: Conditional Access geo-based block policy does not block sign-in from blacklisted country/IP. Attacker still accesses Exchange Online despite CA policy.
rootCause: Legacy/basic auth apps (e.g., iOS default Mail, AppId bfc44fc5-...) use OrgID traffic. CA is skipped for Exchange Server OrgID traffic. Only modern auth evaluated by CA.
solution: Mitigations: (1) app-based CA, (2) disable basic auth via Set-AuthenticationPolicy, (3) Set-OrganizationConfig -IPListBlocked, (4) Client Access Rules in EXO, (5) ADFS claim rules to block non-modern auth, (6) Set-SPOTenant -LegacyAuthProtocolsEnabled false.

symptom: ADFS Extranet Smart Lockout (ESL) cmdlets (Get-ADFSAccountActivity, Set-ADFSAccountActivity, Reset-ADFSAccountLockout) fail when executed remotely, even with domain admin credentials. Error: Only AD FS Service can access this endpoint. The client was authenticated as NT AUTHORITY ANONYMOUS LOGON. Event ID 1100 in ADFS Admin log.
rootCause: ESL REST endpoint requires ADFS local administrator privilege executed locally on the primary ADFS server. Remote PSH uses IWA auth then ESL cmdlets reconnect to the REST endpoint using IWA again (double-hop), causing authentication to fail. This is by design in ADFS delegation model.
solution: Use PowerShell Just Enough Administration (JEA) to delegate ESL commands to service desk workers: 1) Create JEA session configuration allowing only ESL cmdlets; 2) Register the JEA endpoint on the primary ADFS server; 3) Service desk connects via Enter-PSSession to the JEA endpoint.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/ADFS and WAP/Case Study/ADFS - Using Powershell _Just Enough Administratio.md

symptom: ADFS federation service fails to start after replacing SSL certificate. ADFS Admin log shows error 356: Failed to register notification to the SQL database. ADFS 2.0/2.1 on Windows Server 2012 (without R2) with SQL configuration database.
rootCause: ADFS 2.0/2.1 has no Set-AdfsSslCertificate cmdlet; certificate replacement must be done manually via IIS console. Additionally SQL Service Broker may be disabled on the configuration database preventing ADFS from registering change notifications.
solution: 1) Use IIS Management Console to replace ADFS SSL cert (not PowerShell). 2) For PS commands on ADFS 2.0/2.1: Get-PSSnapin -Registered then Add-PSSnapin Microsoft.Adfs.PowerShell. 3) Check SQL Service Broker: SELECT is_broker_enabled FROM sys.databases WHERE name=AdfsConfiguration. 4) If disabled: ALTER DATABASE AdfsConfiguration SET ENABLE_BROKER WITH ROLLBACK IMMEDIATE.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/ADFS and WAP/Case Study/ADFS - [Case Sharing] 119033019781878 ADFS fail to.md

symptom: Chrome and Firefox cannot access ADFS IDP-initiated page with connection reset error. IE works fine. Network trace shows Client Hello is immediately RST by ADFS server. SSL ETL shows: none of the cipher suites supported by the client application are supported by the server.
rootCause: A local Group Policy SSL Cipher Suite Order was configured on the ADFS server with a restrictive cipher suite list that excluded cipher suites supported by Chrome/Firefox. IE succeeded because it supports TLS_RSA_WITH_AES_128_CBC_SHA256 which was in the list.
solution: 1) On ADFS server open gpedit.msc - Computer Configuration - Administrative Templates - Network - SSL Configuration Settings. 2) Double-click SSL Cipher Suite Order and set to Disabled (or fix the order). 3) Run gpupdate /force. 4) Restart ADFS server.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/ADFS and WAP/Case Study/ADFS - [Case Sharing] 119040822000085 ADFS 2016 浏览.md

symptom: Login failure in 2-farm ADFS scenario (account provider + resource provider, both ADFS 2016) when using IE11 on Win10/Server 2016 or Edge on Win10. Works in Chrome, Win7 IE11, InPrivate mode, or with Fiddler. Event 364: Token Binding claim in token must match the binding provided by the channel. Event 441: A token with a bad token binding key was found.
rootCause: Known bug in ADFS 2016 Token Binding feature. In 2-farm scenarios, the Token Binding ID from the SAML token does not match the Request Provided ID from the HTTP request. IE11/Edge on Win10 sends Sec-Token-Binding header which triggers the issue; Chrome and older browsers do not support Token Binding so they work fine.
solution: Disable Token Binding on ADFS 2016: Set-AdfsProperties -EnableTokenBinding $false, then restart ADFS service. PG confirmed this is a known issue and Token Binding feature is being deprecated.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/ADFS and WAP/Case Study/ADFS - [Case Sharing] ADFS Token Binding issue -11.md

symptom: ADFS integration with RSA Authentication Agent (custom MFA provider) fails to start secondary auth process for iOS and Mac OS platform. Error: No style sheet is configured in the active theme for default locale [en-GB/2057]. Windows platform works fine.
rootCause: System locale mismatch between ADFS service account and the third-party MFA agent. ADFS service using EN-GB locale while RSA agent configured for EN-US only. iOS/Mac detect locale from server while Windows may handle it differently.
solution: Align system locale: 1) Verify ADFS service account. 2) Get SID: Get-ADUser -Identity service_account | select SID. 3) Configure the ADFS service account to use the same system locale as the RSA Authentication Agent by modifying user profile registry entries.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/ADFS and WAP/Case Study/ADFS - [Case Sharing] System locale failed in inte.md

symptom: Chrome cannot access ADFS sign-in page from external network (via WAP/ADFS proxy). IE and other browsers work fine externally and internally. ADFS proxy system event log: A fatal alert was generated, TLS fatal error code 20. SSL ETL: SslDecryptPacket() FAILED: 0x8009002c.
rootCause: After a Windows update to Windows Server 2012 R2, the ADFS proxy server negotiated a weak cipher suite (e.g. TLS_RSA_WITH_3DES_EDE_CBC_SHA) with Chrome. Chrome dropped support for 3DES ciphers causing TLS handshake failure.
solution: 1) Disable weak cipher suites (especially 3DES-based) on the ADFS proxy/WAP server. 2) Ensure strong cipher suites like TLS_ECDHE_RSA_WITH_AES_256_GCM_SHA384 are enabled and prioritized. 3) Use IISCrypto tool or GPO to manage cipher suite order.

symptom: Cross-forest users can sign in to ADFS with DomainBIOS\User but fail with UPN. Error: incorrect username and password. Environment: multiple AAD instances federated with single ADFS. ADFS debug log shows domain location failure; Netlogon log shows error code 1355.
rootCause: UPN suffix routing is not enabled for the external forest domains. Without UPN suffix routing the NetLogon service cannot locate the correct domain when cross-forest users authenticate with UPN format.
solution: Enable UPN suffix routing for external domains: 1) Open Active Directory Domains and Trusts. 2) Right-click the forest trust - Properties - Name Suffix Routing tab. 3) Enable routing for the UPN suffix of the external forest.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/ADFS and WAP/Case Study/ADFS - [Case Sharing] [119042623000069]Cross Fores.md

symptom: Application requires HS256 (symmetric key) JWT token signing algorithm from ADFS 2016 but gets RS256 signed tokens instead. No documentation about supported signing algorithms.
rootCause: ADFS 2016 only supports RS256 (RSA-SHA256) for JWT token signing using the private key of the ADFS token signing certificate. HS256 (HMAC-SHA256 with symmetric key) is not supported. Confirmed by PG but not documented publicly.
solution: Redesign the application to accept RS256 signed JWT tokens from ADFS. If HS256 is absolutely required consider using a different IdP or adding a token transformation layer. No workaround on ADFS side.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/ADFS and WAP/Case Study/ADFS - [Case sharing] Supported JWT token signing.md

symptom: Third-party desktop app prompts for credentials when authenticating via ADFS SSO, while browser-based access works fine.
rootCause: ADFS ExtendedProtectionTokenCheck set to Required blocks desktop apps not using Schannel for SSL from WIA authentication.
solution: Set-ADFSProperties -ExtendedProtectionTokenCheck None. Alt: enable Form-based auth and modify app User Agent string.

symptom: All O365 Basic Auth clients unable to access email. Modern Auth and OWA work fine. ADFS external auth fails on usernamemixed endpoint.
rootCause: ADFS Extranet Lockout depends on PDC. When PDC unavailable, lockout blocks all external Basic Auth. Debug logs show AccountLockoutPolicy errors.
solution: Disable Extranet Lockout: Set-AdfsProperties -EnableExtranetLockout $false. Fix PDC/Kerberos. Diagnostic: test IDP-initiated page internally vs externally.

symptom: Cannot convert federated domain to managed after ADFS server uninstalled. Set-MsolADFSContext fails.
rootCause: ADFS server removed before domain conversion. Standard cmdlets require active ADFS.
solution: Use Set-MsolDomainAuthentication or Get-MsolUser | Convert-MsolFederatedUser directly without ADFS context. Passwords lost after conversion.

rootCause: New-MsolFederatedDomain only works with AD FS as IDP. Fails for 3rd-party IdPs.
solution: Use New-MsolDomain -Name child.domain -Authentication Federated. For non-ADFS: use New-MsolDomain, Set-MsolDomainAuthentication, Set-MsolDomainFederationSettings.

symptom: Update-MsolFederatedDomain -SupportMultipleDomain fails. IssuerUri mismatch between ADFS service identifier and domain-based URI.
rootCause: First federated domain set up without -SupportMultipleDomain. HAADJ script with multipleVerifiedDomainNames=true adds claim rule changing issuerURI format.
solution: Delete claim rules, rerun with multipleVerifiedDomainNames=false. Or reconfigure with -SupportMultipleDomain. Check via Get-MsolDomainFederationSettings.

rootCause: Mismatch between signing cert in SAML request and cert in ADFS RP Trust config. RP vendor using wrong cert.
solution: Verify ADFS via IDP-initiated page. Capture Fiddler + Debug logs. Compare certs. RP vendor must fix signing cert.

symptom: ADFS Health Agent synthetic transactions fail with 503 on /adfs/probe. Test-AdfsServerHealth reports failure.
rootCause: /adfs/probe endpoint not listened. Known .NET Framework bug (fixed in 4.7).
solution: Restart AD FS service. Upgrade .NET to 4.7+. Use ETL trace script to diagnose.

symptom: SAML authentication to ADFS RP fails when POST binding is used and the SAML request does not contain a signing certificate. ADFS uses only the first certificate from the RP Trust signature certificate array (unsorted from DB).
rootCause: For POST binding, ADFS expects the signing certificate embedded in the SAML request. If missing, ADFS picks the first cert from the database (not sorted). If that cert does not match the one used by the RP to sign, validation fails.
solution: Ensure RP includes signing certificate in the SAML request body. For Redirect binding, ADFS scans all certs. For POST binding without embedded cert, verify the first cert in ADFS RP Trust matches the RP signing cert.

symptom: adfshelp.microsoft.com GenerateClaims tool produces invalid RegEx for IssuerURI claim rule when multiple federated domains include subdomains registered before parent domain.
rootCause: Bug in adfshelp tool: generates RegEx with consecutive asterisks instead of pipe separator when complex subdomain hierarchy exists.
solution: Validate generated RegEx at regex101.com before deploying. Fix by replacing consecutive asterisks with pipe separator. Manually verify domain-to-IssuerURI mapping.

symptom: WAP client certificate authentication fails with CERT_E_UNTRUSTEDROOT (-2146762487) when SendTrustedIssuerList=0; or no client cert provided when SendTrustedIssuerList=1.
rootCause: Non-self-signed certificate (e.g., Intermediate CA) installed in Local Computer Trusted Root Certification Authorities store on WAP server corrupts the trusted issuer list.
solution: Remove/move non-self-signed certificates from Local Computer Trusted Root Certification Authorities store on WAP. Ref: KB2802568, KB2795828.

symptom: WAP configuration wizard fails with 401 Unauthorized. WAP proxy cannot download configuration from ADFS server.
rootCause: Exact IP:port SSL bindings on ADFS server take precedence over ADFS hostname:port binding. The IP binding uses default Trusted Root CA store (not AdfsTrustedDevices), so WAP self-signed client cert fails validation.
solution: Remove exact IP:port SSL bindings on ADFS/WAP servers. Add IP wildcard binding: netsh http add sslcert ipport=0.0.0.0:443 certhash=<hash> appid={5d89a20c-beab-4389-9447-324788eb944a} for non-SNI client support.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/ADFS and WAP/Case Study/ADFS-[ Sharing] [REG_115042312669490] Client certi.md

symptom: ADFS service fails to start after SSL certificate replacement. Event 133: certificate private key cannot be accessed. CNG key error.
rootCause: ADFS does not support CNG (Cryptographic Next Generation) keys. Certificate with ProviderType=0 and KeySpec=0 is CNG-keyed.
solution: Replace certificate with one NOT using CNG keys. Check with: certutil -v -store my <serial>. If ProviderType=0 and KeySpec=0 -> CNG keyed -> not supported by ADFS.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/ADFS and WAP/Case Study/ADFS-[Case Sharing] ADFS fail to start due to cert.md

symptom: ADFS service running but /adfs/services/trust/mex returns 503. Office client SSO fails. IDP-initiated sign-on works. Error: certificate not capable of key exchange.
rootCause: Certificate KeySpec=2 (AT_SIGNATURE) instead of AT_KEYEXCHANGE. Cannot be seen in certificate UI, only via certutil -v -store my.
solution: Delete certificate and reimport with: certutil -importpfx certfile.pfx AT_KEYEXCHANGE. No need for new certificate.

symptom: ADFS alternate login ID with Employee Number fails. Set-AdfsClaimsProviderTrust succeeds but authentication with employee number (e.g., 5555) fails.
rootCause: Alternate login ID attribute must conform to UPN format (e.g., 5555@domain.com). Plain digits are not valid. Attribute must also be indexed, in Global Catalog, and GC-replicated.
solution: 1) Ensure AD attribute is GC-replicated (AD Schema snap-in, check Replicate this attribute to the Global Catalog). 2) Format employee number as UPN: 5555@domain.com. 3) Workaround: customize ADFS onload.js to auto-append domain suffix for non-UPN input.

symptom: One specific federated user cannot access SharePoint Online. SAML token IssuerURI contains trailing newline. Other O365 apps work for same user.
rootCause: User UPN in Active Directory stored in Base64-encoded format (ldifde export shows encoded value). The encoded UPN contains trailing newline character which gets embedded in SAML token IssuerURI.
solution: Revert user UPN in Active Directory from Base64-encoded format to normal plain text UPN. Verify via ldifde -f export.txt -r samaccountname=<user>.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/ADFS and WAP/Case Study/ADFS-[Case Sharing] _ #118100219133650 _ ADFS issu.md

symptom: ADFS Relying Party requires claim value (e.g., username) in all uppercase. No built-in ADFS function for string case conversion.
rootCause: ADFS claim language has no native toUpper/toLower function. Requires custom attribute store DLL.
solution: Deploy StringProcessingAttributeStore DLL to C:\windows\ADFS on all ADFS servers. Register as Custom Attribute Store. Use claim rule with query=toUpper. NOTE: input claim type must match previous rule output type; store name and query must match DLL code exactly (case-sensitive).
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/ADFS and WAP/Case Study/ADFS-[Case Sharing] _ #118110519304247 _ Passing t.md

symptom: ADFS Windows Integrated Authentication (WIA) fails for workgroup (non-domain-joined) machines accessing O365 from intranet. ADFS attempts Kerberos auth which fails for workgroup machines.
rootCause: ADFS checks WIASupportedUserAgents property against the client user agent. If matched, ADFS attempts WIA (Kerberos). Workgroup machines cannot perform Kerberos auth, causing failure.
solution: Use Group Policy to set a specific custom user agent on domain-joined machines, then restrict ADFS WIASupportedUserAgents to only that custom agent. This way workgroup machines fall back to forms auth.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/ADFS and WAP/Case Study/ADFS-[case sharing]INTRANET SSO.md

symptom: User is prompted with login form instead of SSO in intranet when accessing O365 through federated ADFS. Expected behavior is Windows Integrated Authentication (silent SSO).
rootCause: The application sends prompt=login parameter to AAD. AAD translates this to wauth=urn:oasis:names:tc:SAML:1.0:am:password and wfresh=0, which forces ADFS to use Forms Authentication instead of WIA.
solution: Set ADFS PromptLoginBehavior to Disabled so ADFS ignores the wauth/wfresh parameters translated from prompt=login. Also ensure: DNS A record for ADFS, correct SPN, and ADFS URL in IE Local Intranet Zone (https: not http:).
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/ADFS and WAP/Case Study/ADFS-[case sharing]prompt=login parameter.md

symptom: ADFS configuration to authenticate users stored in AD LDS (LDAP directories) fails when using Get-Credential approach from official documentation.
rootCause: The official Microsoft documentation PowerShell example for creating LDAP server connection uses Get-Credential incorrectly for AD LDS scenarios. The doc approach does not work with AD LDS.
solution: Use PSCredential directly: $ldapuser='CN=admin,OU=...'; $ldappassword=ConvertTo-SecureString -String 'pwd' -AsPlainText -Force; $Credential=New-Object System.Management.Automation.PSCredential -ArgumentList $ldapuser,$ldappassword. Then use $Credential in New-AdfsLdapServerConnection.

symptom: ADFS LDAP query fails when UserContainer OU name contains a space (e.g. OU=User Container). ADFS URL-encodes the space as %20 which LDAP server cannot resolve.
rootCause: Known ADFS bug: ADFS incorrectly URL-encodes spaces in LDAP UserContainer parameter, translating spaces to %20 in the LDAP query, which the LDAP server cannot interpret.
solution: No workaround available. Known bug reported to ADFS product team. Avoid spaces in OU names if possible.

symptom: Update-MsolFederatedDomain fails with 'Invalid length for a Base-64 char array or string' when trying to update O365 RPT token-signing certificate. ADFS metadata is not publicly accessible.
rootCause: When ADFS metadata is not publicly accessible from the internet, the O365 RPT cannot auto-update the token-signing cert. The Update-MsolFederatedDomain cmdlet fails with base64 parsing errors.
solution: Use Set-MsolDomainFederationSettings as workaround: 1) Export token-signing cert from ADFS console in base64 format, 2) Read cert file, strip BEGIN/END headers and newlines, 3) Run Set-MsolDomainFederationSettings -DomainName 'domain' -SigningCertificate '$cert'. Can run from any PC.

symptom: ADFS Smart Lockout PowerShell cmdlets return 401 Unauthorized error on ADFS 4.0 (Windows Server 2016). ESL works but management cmdlets fail.
rootCause: Duplicate SPN registered on two different AD accounts. ADFS internal REST API calls /adfs/users/ endpoint. Kerberos auth fails due to duplicate SPN, falling back to NTLM which is denied.
solution: Check for duplicate SPNs using setspn -Q. Remove the duplicate SPN registration from the incorrect account. After removing duplicate, ADFS management cmdlets work correctly.

symptom: New-MgDomainFederationConfiguration fails with 403 Forbidden (Authorization_RequestDenied) when using app-only auth with Domain.ReadWrite.All permission. Converting managed domain to federated fails.
rootCause: SetDomainAuthentication action requires Domain-InternalFederation.ReadWrite.All or IdentityProvider.ReadWrite.All, NOT Domain.ReadWrite.All. MS Learn doc incorrectly lists Domain.ReadWrite.All as sufficient.
solution: Add Domain-InternalFederation.ReadWrite.All (or IdentityProvider.ReadWrite.All) to the app registration. Verify via DSExplorer that the required permission maps to SetDomainAuthentication action. ICM: 670746109.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/ADFS and WAP/Case Study/Aode [ADFS]Domain.readWrite.All is not sufficient.md

symptom: WAP trust rebuild fails immediately with 'Could not create SSL/TLS secure channel' after sudden WAP service crash. All standard checks pass. Network trace shows successful TLS 1.2 handshake.
rootCause: SCHANNEL registry key DisableRenegoOnServer=1 on ADFS server. WAP trust uses client certificate auth performed during TLS renegotiation. Disabling renegotiation blocks WAP from authenticating to ADFS after trust establishment.
solution: Set HKLM\System\CurrentControlSet\Control\SecurityProviders\SCHANNEL\DisableRenegoOnServer to 0 or delete the key. Diagnose with SCHANNEL ETL trace: look for SEC_E_ILLEGAL_MESSAGE errors indicating blocked renegotiation.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/ADFS and WAP/Case Study/WAP-[case sharing] AAD - Tricky WAP Trust issue.md

symptom: D365 Finance & Operations users must enter different account formats across multiple login pages (D365->AAD->ADFS->custom). UPN domain differs from email domain, federated domain is email domain.
rootCause: D365 only passes tenant info (not domain) to AAD. AAD needs domain to route to ADFS. UPN domain registered in international tenant but removed from 21V during CC B2B migration. Email domain used as federated domain. ADFS not configured with AlternateLoginID.
solution: WA1: Configure ADFS AlternateLoginID (Set-AdfsClaimsProviderTrust -AlternateLoginID mail -LookupForests domain). WA2: Extend SSO with KMSI persistent cookie. D365 token=1 day, AAD refresh token=14d/90d max.

symptom: Mooncake myapps portal (myapps.windowsazure.cn) cannot launch SAML SSO applications. Error AADSTS50011: redirect URI mismatch for myapps application.
rootCause: Mooncake myapps portal does not support launching SAML SSO apps the same way as global AAD. The myapps app registration in Mooncake does not have correct redirect URIs.
solution: Forge SAML sign-in URL manually: 1) Capture SAMLRequest from global AAD test login via F12. 2) Decode at samltool.com/decode.php. 3) Modify AssertionConsumerServiceURL (=reply URL), Issuer (=Entity ID), IssueInstant. 4) Re-encode and URL-encode. 5) Construct: https://login.partner.microsoftonline.cn/{tenant}/saml2?SAMLRequest={encoded}. Reply URL in Mooncake: App registration > Authentication > Redirect URIs. Entity ID: Expose an API > Application ID URI.

symptom: Graph API servicePrincipal AddKey operation fails silently. MSODS logs show no clear error. Requires proof of possession token.
rootCause: AddKey API requires proof of possession (PoP) token from existing valid cert on SP. If no existing cert or PoP token incorrectly generated, operation fails silently.
solution: 1) Generate PoP token per MS Learn doc. 2) Install NuGet packages, configure parameters. 3) Alternative: use PATCH with full keyCredentials/passwordCredentials instead of AddKey. 4) Debug via Kusto IfxUlsEvents table with SP objectId for internalCorrelationId.

symptom: Azure web app fails to get access token. AADSTS50194: Application not configured as multi-tenant. /common endpoint not supported for apps created after 10/15/2018.
rootCause: Single-tenant app created after 10/15/2018 cannot use /common endpoint. Microsoft enforced this restriction from that date. Older apps with same config work fine.
solution: Either: 1) Use tenant-specific endpoint (login.microsoftonline.com/{tenantId}/...) instead of /common, or 2) Configure app as multi-tenant in App Registration > Authentication.

symptom: AWS SAML SSO fails with invalid SAML response error on AWS side after setup. AAD shows no errors. Provisioning shows no export activity.
rootCause: User assigned Default Access role instead of provisioned AWS role. AWS SSO requires Role and RoleSessionName claims with actual AWS IAM roles. AWS user provisioning from AAD NOT supported - only role sync (AWS->AAD). AWS uses JIT provisioning for users.
solution: 1) Configure auto-provisioning to sync AWS roles to AAD (not users). 2) Assign users with actual AWS role (synced) instead of Default Access. 3) Ensure Role and RoleSessionName claims configured in SAML attributes.

symptom: Password-based SSO browser extension cannot capture sign-in fields for non-gallery application. Login page loads but fields not detected.
rootCause: Sign-in page uses iframe to embed login form. Password SSO field capture only supports standard HTML pages, not iframes, Flash, or non-HTML technologies.
solution: Use direct sign-in URL (iframe src URL) that renders login form without iframe wrapper. Check with F12 if login page has iframe elements. Configure app with direct URL as sign-on URL.

symptom: Picturepark SAML-based SSO integration fails. SSO does not work after following the official Microsoft tutorial for Picturepark SAML SSO configuration.
rootCause: Picturepark only supports WS-Federation protocol, not SAML. The official Microsoft documentation incorrectly provides SAML SSO configuration steps for Picturepark.
solution: Change the SAML endpoint to WS-Federation endpoint in the Azure AD enterprise application configuration. Configure Picturepark to use the WS-Fed endpoint URL instead of the SAML endpoint. All other configurations from the tutorial remain valid after the endpoint change.

symptom: AAD enterprise application does not appear in My Apps Portal after assigning users. App registration uses wildcard reply URLs (e.g., http://localhost:3000/*, https://dev.example.com/*).
rootCause: By design. Wildcard reply URLs have limited support in Azure AD v1 and are not supported in v2. Apps with only wildcard reply URLs will not show in My Apps Portal. The new App Registration blade treats wildcards as illegal.
solution: Remove wildcard reply URLs and replace with exact redirect URIs. Wildcards increase security surface area (XSS, referrer header leaks). In the new App Registration blade, wildcards cannot be saved.

symptom: Customer wants to use dynamic parameters in redirect URI (e.g., https://xxx.com?aaa={a}&bbb={b}). Wildcard redirect URIs with query parameters do not work as expected.
rootCause: Azure AD redirect URI wildcard matching has limitations: wildcards (*) can match path characters but cannot contain ? or & characters. Supported wildcard formats are: https://*.domain.com and https://www.domain.com/* only.
solution: Use supported wildcard formats only: https://*.mstest.com or https://www.mstest.com/*. For dynamic parameters, handle them in the application code after receiving the redirect. To add wildcard redirect URIs, use the application manifest editor. Ref: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity-platform/reply-url#restrictions-on-wildcards-in-redirect-uris

rootCause: The user account does not have the required directory role (Global Administrator, Application Administrator, or Cloud Application Administrator). The portal overview links to Enterprise Applications directly, making users think they can add apps, but the button is greyed out without proper roles.
solution: Assign the appropriate directory role (Global Admin, Application Admin, or Cloud Application Admin) to the user account. The issue resolves immediately after role assignment. Tip: always do a remote session to verify UI state rather than relying solely on logs.

symptom: Need to allow a specific user to grant admin consent only for a specific app (App B) to access a specific API (App A exposed delegation permission), without granting Cloud App Admin role.
rootCause: By default, granting admin consent requires at least Cloud App Admin role, which is too broad. Azure AD supports custom app consent policies to scope admin consent to specific app-to-app combinations.
solution: Create custom app consent policy: 1) New-MgPolicyPermissionGrantPolicy with specific client/resource app IDs. 2) New-MgPolicyPermissionGrantPolicyInclude for conditions. 3) Create custom Entra ID role with microsoft.directory/servicePrincipals/managePermissionGrantsForAll.{policyId}. 4) Assign role to user, wait ~15 min. For 21V: Connect-MgGraph -Environment China.
