symptom: SAML SSO fails with AADSTS75011: Authentication method WindowsIntegrated does not match requested authentication method Password, ProtectedTransport.
rootCause: SaaS app SAML request has RequestedAuthnContext Comparison="exact" requesting PasswordProtectedTransport (forms-based auth). Internal users authenticate via Windows Integrated Authentication (WIA), causing exact comparison to fail.
solution: Contact SaaS app vendor to: 1) Change RequestedAuthnContext Comparison from "exact" to less restrictive value, or 2) Allow other authentication methods in SAML request besides PasswordProtectedTransport. Verify by checking SAMLRequest in Fiddler for RequestedAuthnContext element.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/Application mgmt and config/SaaS Application-[case sharing]AAD SAAS - Access m.md

symptom: Azure AD to SaaS app (e.g., Salesforce) provisioning syncs unexpected/incorrect timezone for user accounts.
rootCause: Azure AD user accounts do not have a timezone attribute. The attribute mapping for TimeZoneSidKey is missing or incorrectly configured in enterprise application provisioning settings.
solution: Edit or create attribute mapping: Enterprise app > Provisioning > Mappings. For TimeZoneSidKey: Mapping type=None, Default value=desired timezone (e.g., Australia/Sydney), Target attribute=TimeZoneSidKey, Apply=Always. If not listed, click Add New Mapping.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/Application mgmt and config/SaaS Application-[case sharing]AAD SAAS - Applicat.md

symptom: Google App provisioning configuration fails to save with error: Size of input ExternalSecret data is invalid. Credentials could not be saved due to internal storage issue.
rootCause: Combined configuration data (credentials + settings like notification email) exceeds Azure AD internal storage limit for ExternalSecret.
solution: Two workarounds: 1) Use two separate gallery app instances - one for SSO, one for provisioning, to reduce per-app storage. 2) Remove optional fields like Notification Email from provisioning page to reduce data size.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/Application mgmt and config/SaaS Application-[case sharing]AAD SAAS - Google A.md

symptom: AADSTS65005: Invalid resource error when accessing SaaS application via SAML SSO. Client requested access to resource not listed in requested permissions.
rootCause: The Identifier (Entity ID) URL was not configured in the Enterprise Application SSO advanced URL settings. The SAML request Issuer from application side did not match any configured identifier.
solution: Go to Enterprise Application SSO configuration page > Advanced URL settings, manually enter the correct Identifier URL matching the SAML request Issuer. Use Fiddler to capture SAML request and verify the Issuer value.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/Application mgmt and config/SaaS Application-[case sharing]AAD SAAS - Invalid.md

symptom: Enterprise application SAML SSO failure with redirect loop. User sign-in redirects cyclically between two URLs.
rootCause: Duplicate application registrations in the directory. The configured Enterprise Application was not the correct one with the proper attribute mapping. Identifier URL mismatch between duplicate apps.
solution: 1) Check App Registrations for duplicate apps with same name. 2) Delete duplicate registrations. 3) Verify the correct app has proper identifier and attribute configuration. 4) For multi-tenant apps that cannot be deleted, set availableToOtherTenants=false in Manifest first.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/Application mgmt and config/SaaS Application-[case sharing]AAD SAAS - Single s.md

symptom: AADSTS65001: User or administrator has not consented to use the application. Web app fails to get access token silently after deployment.
rootCause: Admin consent was not granted for the app. Silent token acquisition (no user interaction) cannot prompt interactive consent. Consent status can be verified via MSODS Explorer delegation entries.
solution: Grant admin consent via Azure Portal Grant Permissions button. Verify consent with MSODS Explorer delegation entries (Source=app SP, Target=resource SP). Use eSTS Logs Viewer/LogsMiner to confirm DelegationDoesNotExist error stopped. Track who granted consent via Jarvis MSODS activity logs using Service Principal Object ID.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/Application mgmt and config/SaaS Application-[case sharing]AAD SAAS - Tracking.md

symptom: SaaS application (Replicon) SAML SSO fails with Azure AD. Error shows permission issue from a different/unknown tenant.
rootCause: The Identifier URL in SAML request was missing a path segment (/saml2/). Mismatch between app-configured identifier and Azure AD Enterprise App identifier caused authentication to hit wrong tenant.
solution: Capture Fiddler trace of SAML SSO flow. Compare SAML request identifier with Enterprise App SSO configuration. Fix the identifier URL to include the missing path segment. Use Test SAML SSO feature in Azure Portal to validate.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/Application mgmt and config/SaaS Application-[case sharing]AAD SAAS- Replicon.md

symptom: Optional claims customization not taking effect or being overwritten. Claims configured in Azure Portal SSO page show warning about being overridden by claims mapping policy.
rootCause: Azure AD has 3 hierarchical levels for claims customization: Level 1 (App Manifest optional claims) < Level 2 (Enterprise App SSO portal config/ClaimIssuancePolicy) < Level 3 (Claims Mapping Policy via Graph/PowerShell). Higher levels override lower ones. IncludeBasicClaimSet=false in ClaimIssuancePolicy removes basic claims unless individually added.
solution: Check all 3 levels: 1) App Registration > Token configuration (manifest). 2) Enterprise App > SSO > Attributes & Claims (backed by ClaimIssuancePolicy). 3) Claims Mapping Policy via PowerShell Get-AzureADPolicy. If Level 3 policy exists, portal config (Level 2) is ignored. Remove claims mapping policy or configure all claims at same level.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/Application mgmt and config/Training Material/Optional Claims.md

symptom: AADSTS90033: A transient error has occurred when performing admin consent to web app. Error persists for days despite retries.
rootCause: Application has too many API permissions configured (25+). Directory Proxy (DPX) has a 5-second timeout threshold. EvoSTS queries each API permission in directory; combined lookup time exceeds timeout when app objects are remote to request landing site.
solution: Reduce the number of API permissions per consent request or use Dynamic consent to request permissions incrementally. If issue persists, escalate to EvoSTS/DPX team via ICM. DPX timeout is a known architectural limitation.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/Application mgmt and config/[Quick Sharing]  Unable to admin consent to web ap.md

symptom: ADFS Extranet Lockout feature does not work for user accounts from a trusted (resource) domain. Only accounts from the ADFS farm's own domain are soft-locked.
rootCause: Extranet Lockout requires PDC of the user's domain to be reachable. Cross-domain scenarios fail when PDC is unavailable or DNS resolution between domains is broken. The feature only protects against brute-force via WAP (extranet); it does not cover intranet auth.
solution: 1) Ensure PDC of each trusted domain is reachable from ADFS servers. 2) Check ADFS Audit log event IDs: 512 (ExtranetLockoutAccountThrottledAudit), 515 (ExtranetLockoutUserThrottleTransitionAudit), 516 (ExtranetLockoutAccountRestrictedAudit). 3) Verify settings: ExtranetObservationWindow > AD 'Reset Account Lockout Counter After'; ExtranetLockoutThreshold < AD 'Account Lockout Threshold'.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/ADFS and WAP/Feature_specific configuration/Extranet lockout.md

symptom: Guest user cross-tenant SSO shows GUID as NameID instead of email address. NameID format is persistent with random GUID.
rootCause: Claims mapping policies do not apply to guest users (B2B). For B2B users, mapping NameID and UPN cross-tenant is prevented for security reasons. The mail claim is on the NameID cross-tenant block list.
solution: Cannot use mail as NameID for guest users via claims mapping. Change the application to SP-initiated and configure NameID format request in SAML request as emailAddress format.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/Application mgmt and config/application token-[case sharing] Cross-tenant acce.md

symptom: Refresh token renewed despite MaxInactiveTime exceeded and Outlook was idle/closed longer than configured.
rootCause: Outlook requests tokens for multiple SPs (Exchange + OfficeClientService). Token Lifetime Policy only applied to Exchange SP. Relaunching Outlook first requests token for OfficeClientService (no policy), renewing the refresh token.
solution: Apply Token Lifetime Policy to ALL service principals the client may request tokens for. Use Jarvis/ESTS ITData logs to identify which ResourceIDs are accessed.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/Application mgmt and config/refresh token-[case sharing]   get details of refr.md

symptom: Az CLI login fails with AADSTS53003 blocked by CA even though browser auth succeeds from same machine.
rootCause: Browser uses PAC-file proxy (corporate IP in CA trusted locations), but Az CLI does not inherit proxy. Az CLI sends token redemption directly without proxy, using different IP not in trusted locations.
solution: Configure proxy env vars before az login: HTTP_PROXY and HTTPS_PROXY. Also check WinHTTP proxy (netsh winhttp show proxy).
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/Conditional Access/Case study/Az CLI received AADSTS53003_ Access has been block.md

symptom: AADSTS50076 MFA required unexpectedly when accessing PIM in Azure portal. Blue screen session expired targeting Microsoft Graph.
rootCause: PIM uses .default scope evaluating all pre-authorized scopes. Some scopes (RoleManagement.Read.Directory, Group.Read.All) are unmapped in ESTS. Unmapped scopes default CA evaluation to EXO/SPO. CA policy requiring MFA for SPO triggers for PIM.
solution: Expected behavior per CA PG. Workaround: exclude PIM users from SPO MFA CA policy, or escalate to PIM/Portal PG to handle MFA challenge.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/Conditional Access/Case study/CA evaluation on EXO and SPO caused by unmapped sc.md

symptom: ADFS token signing certificate recently renewed but relying party (AAD or app) still has old cert public key. SAML/WS-Fed signature verification fails. SSO breaks for federated users.
rootCause: ADFS signs tokens with new token signing cert private key after auto-renewal, but relying party (AAD, or another SP) hasn't fetched the updated metadata containing the new cert public key. Signature verification fails.
solution: 1) Update relying party with new cert: for AAD run Update-MsolFederatedDomain; for other SPs update their metadata endpoint or manually import the new cert. 2) Check ADFS metadata endpoint is accessible: https://<adfs>/FederationMetadata/2007-06/FederationMetadata.xml. 3) Consider disabling AutoCertificateRollover if causing recurrent issues.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/ADFS and WAP/Log collection_tools/Start with ADFS troubleshooting.md

symptom: Customer asks about AAD optional claims enfpolids and in_corp — unclear what they represent and how they work.
rootCause: enfpolids = Object ID of Conditional Access policy hit during sign-in, mainly for MS internal services, not useful for external app developers. in_corp = signal indicating if client is logging in from corporate network, determined by MFA trusted IPs list and Named Locations marked as trusted, NOT by ADFS/customer internal network definition.
solution: Explain enfpolids is CA policy object ID (useless for non-MS apps, no Graph/PowerShell query available). Explain in_corp is based on MFA trusted IPs and Named Locations, not ADFS network boundary. Ref: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory/develop/active-directory-optional-claims
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/Application mgmt and config/AAD Optional Claims - [quick Sharing] Question abo.md

symptom: Auto-provisioning (SCIM) for non-gallery app fails with SystemForCrossDomainIdentityManagementCredentialValidationFailure. Test Connection intermittently returns 'Invalid credentials' or succeeds.
rootCause: The SCIM endpoint URL provided by the app vendor has an invalid/mismatched SSL certificate (e.g., cert for *.lastpass.com but URL is different subdomain). Azure AD SCIM provisioning agent rejects untrusted remote certificate during TLS handshake. Jarvis log shows: 'The remote certificate is invalid according to the validation procedure'.
solution: Use the correct SCIM endpoint URL that has a valid SSL certificate. For LastPass example: change from old URL format to https://lastpass.com/scimapi/{accountId}. Verify the URL's SSL cert in browser before configuring. Restart full sync after URL change.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/Application mgmt and config/Case sharing  _ Auto Provisioning.md

symptom: Need to export expiration dates of all Service Principal and App Registration secrets/certificates in a 21V (Mooncake) Entra tenant to audit or plan rotation.
rootCause: No built-in portal feature to bulk-view all SP/App credential expiry dates. Manual checking is impractical for large tenants.
solution: Use official PowerShell script from Azure China docs: https://docs.azure.cn/zh-cn/entra/identity/enterprise-apps/scripts/powershell-export-apps-with-expiring-secrets — dedicated for 21V, no modification needed. Alternative: GitHub gist https://gist.github.com/torumakabe/779490238f238d0bece355daddb37b05
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/Application mgmt and config/Case study/Export expiration date of SP_APP.md

symptom: Outlook Report Phishing add-in fails on VDI (VMware) with CAE error: InteractionRequired / LocationConditionEvaluationSatisfied when accessing microsoftgraph.chinacloudapi.cn
rootCause: VDI routes login and Graph traffic through different outbound IPs (firewall vs Zscaler). CAE detects IP change mid-session: token issued from trusted IP but Graph call from untrusted IP.
solution: Option 1: Add VDI outbound IP (Zscaler) to CA trusted IP-based Named Location. Option 2: Fix VDI network so both endpoints use same outbound IP. Debug: decode access token at jwt.ms, base64-decode claims in 401 for xms_rp_ipaddr.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/Conditional Access/Case study/[CAE]  Continuous access evaluation resulted in ch.md

symptom: RDP to AADJ VM fails with internal error when using FQDN for Entra ID web account auth. Succeeds with NetBIOS name or IP.
rootCause: Custom domain FQDN suffix missing ICP record in 21Vianet. Azure China blocks connections to domains without ICP. TCP RST sent by 21v-bluecloud.
solution: Option 1: Complete ICP registration at support.azure.cn. Option 2: Change VM FQDN suffix to domain with ICP record. Workaround: Use NetBIOS name or IP for RDP.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/Device Registration/MC Case Study/2506120050003842 - Fail to RDP with AAD Account.md

symptom: On AADJ machine, users can still login with old password after password change. CachedLogonsCount=0 does not prevent cached login.
rootCause: By design. AADJ uses cloudAP cache separate from on-prem lsass. CachedLogonsCount=0 only affects on-prem. PG aware, no fix planned.
solution: Option 1: Login with new password to update cloudAP cache. Option 2: Delete CloudAPCache folder. Find alias via HKLM IdentityStore LogonCache Alias2Name.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/Device Registration/MC Case Study/Cached logon in AAD joined machine.md

symptom: On HAADJ device (Mooncake), Office client shows cached credentials expired. Fix Me wizard loops. WAM renews PRT/RT but Office uses old expired RT from wrong cache entry.
rootCause: Bug in Win10 2004. WAM writes renewed RT to new cache entry but Office reads from old cache. Authority URL mismatch causes wrong cache lookup. Fixed in Outlook, not SharePoint.
solution: Outlook: Update to latest. OneDrive/SharePoint: PG fix pending. Workaround: WAM diagnostics (aka.ms/wamhot) to clear token cache.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/Device Registration/MC Case Study/SSO - Office client still used expired RT though W.md

symptom: HAADJ device cannot get PRT after OS reimage. Device registration fails with error: The verification of the signature failed. PRT not obtainable until AAD Connect syncs the new UserCertificate.
rootCause: After OS reimage, the device generates a new self-signed UserCertificate, but AAD still has the old certificate. Device registration authentication fails because the certificate mismatch. AAD Connect default sync interval is 30 minutes, and registration task is only triggered by user logon or event 4096.
solution: Wait ~30 minutes for AAD Connect to sync the new UserCertificate to AAD, then re-login to the device to trigger device registration task again. After successful device registration, re-login once more to obtain PRT. If AAD Connect Wizard is open in debug mode, sync will be paused - close it first.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/Device Registration/MC Case Study/Unable to get PRT after OS reimage.md

symptom: After running Convert-MsolDomainToFederated for Mooncake domain, the ADFS relying party configuration for global AAD gets overwritten by Mooncake configuration. Global AAD federation breaks.
rootCause: Old version of MSOnline PowerShell module cannot distinguish public AAD from Mooncake AAD. It searches relying party by name "Microsoft Office 365 Identity Platform" and overwrites the entire configuration. Latest module uses distinct names: "...Worldwide" for public and "...China" for Mooncake.
solution: Always update MSOnline module to latest before running federation commands: Install-Module MSOnline -Force. The latest module creates separate relying party trusts for public ("Worldwide") and Mooncake ("China") AAD, preventing overwrite.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/Dual-Federation/Case study/2111220040001272 Out-of-dated MSOnline module caus.md

symptom: In dual-federation environment with same UPN in both public and 21v clouds, iOS users get "Org Data Removal" prompt in Outlook/PowerApps, or auth fails when mis-selecting wrong cloud credential in Authenticator. Affects all Microsoft SaaS apps on the device.
rootCause: Intune SDK for iOS changed device primary user determination from UPN to OID (since 2022/05/25). In dual-fed environments with same UPN across clouds, the SDK cannot correctly distinguish users, causing MAM policy conflicts and data removal prompts.
solution: Short-term: (1) Use Edge web apps instead of native PowerApps app. (2) Use Teams add-in apps for PowerApps. (3) PG publishes private version under different publisher. Long-term: (1) Use cross-cloud B2B (different UPN for 21v). (2) Change UPN in one cloud by modifying ADFS config + AAD Connect sync rules + on-prem AD attributes. (3) Wait for Intune MAM "Multiple Managed Account" feature.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/Dual-Federation/Case study/Dual-fed with Intune compatibility.md

symptom: AAD issues access token for application whose Service Principal was deleted. Token contains appid but missing oid/puid/altsecid claims. ARM returns InvalidAuthenticationToken error, AAD Graph returns Authorization_IdentityNotFound.
rootCause: By design: AAD can issue client_credentials tokens (ClientOnlyJsonWebToken) for multi-tenant applications even when the service principal does not exist in the tenant. The token is issued based on the application registration in the home tenant, not the local SP.
solution: Recreate the service principal in the tenant: New-AzureADServicePrincipal -AppId <appId>. This is by design per Microsoft documentation on service principal provisioning. Without SP, the token will lack oid claim and be rejected by resource APIs like ARM.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/Application mgmt and config/Case study/Service Principal got deleted but AAD still issues.md

symptom: Numerous AAD audit log failures for 'adding service principal' triggered by 'Windows Azure Service Management API'. Error: appId does not reference a valid application object. Occurs when registering/unregistering resource providers.
rootCause: ARM automatically attempts to create service principals for resource provider apps when processing subscription operations. If the first-party app (e.g., China CDN Resource Provider, Azure Machine Learning Singularity) has not been deployed to Mooncake, the SP creation fails because the application object doesn't exist in the Mooncake directory.
solution: This is expected behavior and can be safely ignored. The audit log failures indicate first-party apps not yet available in Mooncake. No customer action needed. ARM will continue to retry periodically but these failures do not affect other operations.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/Application mgmt and config/Case study/[First Party App] Numerous failure of adding servi.md

symptom: On-premise data gateway login fails because Power BI service application SP gets automatically disabled. SP must be manually re-enabled before each sign-in.
rootCause: Power BI service SP has ServicePrincipalLifecyclePolicy set to SubscriptionManaged(1). Since the tenant has no Power BI license/subscription, the ordering management system periodically disables the SP.
solution: Manually re-enable the SP before each use. PG acknowledges this is a design issue and improvement is on their roadmap. No ETA available. Long-term: customer should acquire PBI license if using data gateway.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/Application mgmt and config/Case study/[First Party App] application “Power BI service”.md

symptom: AADSTS500014: Service principal for resource is disabled. Affects multiple services: Power BI Desktop login, Logic App on-premise data gateway, AIP/MIP service. SP gets disabled unexpectedly and periodically without admin action.
rootCause: First-party apps with ServicePrincipalLifecyclePolicy=SubscriptionManaged(1) are managed by the ordering management system. It periodically checks if the tenant has a paid subscription associated with the service. If no valid subscription found, the SP is disabled. E.g., PBI requires PBI Pro/Premium license, AIP requires EMS/AIP license, MFA requires MFA/AAD Premium license.
solution: 1. Verify customer has valid paid subscription at portal.partner.microsoftonline.cn. 2. Check first-party portal for ServicePrincipalLifecyclePolicy setting. 3. Workaround: Schedule PowerShell task every 30 min to re-enable SP if disabled (Get-MsolServicePrincipal + Set-MsolServicePrincipal -AccountEnabled $true). 4. For AIP Sync service in Mooncake, known bug that it's not bound to any SKU (ICM-281185138).
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/Application mgmt and config/Case study/[TSG] Service principal got disabled unexpectedly.md

symptom: Microsoft Authenticator app cannot handle two accounts with same UPN from different cloud instances (e.g. public Azure and Mooncake). Users cannot register both accounts in one Authenticator app.
rootCause: Authenticator app identifies accounts by UPN and cannot distinguish multiple accounts with the same UPN across different Azure cloud instances.
solution: Register one of the accounts in a different authenticator app (e.g. Google Authenticator). The OTP/TOTP method uses open standard OATH algorithm compatible across authenticator apps.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/Dual-Federation/Technical details/Limitation.md

symptom: PowerApps mobile app fails to authenticate Mooncake account in dual-federation environment when OneAuth is enabled for public tenant.
rootCause: OneAuth feature bug: when enabled for public tenant, PowerApps mobile uses OneAuth instead of Authenticator. OneAuth cannot distinguish dual-fed accounts with same UPN.
solution: (1) Select Use legacy sign-in in Power Apps mobile login UI. (2) Engage PowerApps PG to disable OneAuth feature flag on the tenant. (3) Users must re-install PowerApps mobile app after PG change.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/Dual-Federation/Technical details/Limitation.md

symptom: Custom domain unexpectedly appears in public Azure AD due to viral tenant created by Power BI trial sign-up. Causes dual-federation issues in Mooncake.
rootCause: When a user signs up for PBI trial using corporate email, Azure AD auto-creates a viral tenant in public Azure with that domain, causing it to exist in both clouds unintentionally.
solution: (1) Customer performs admin takeover of viral tenant per MS docs. (2) File ICM (template: m2Q2t1) to block domain adoption BEFORE deleting the domain. (3) Contact Peter Shi / nagendran.sankaralingam to add to adoption blocklist. (4) Verify by testing PBI sign-up - should show personal email.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/Dual-Federation/Viral tenant take over and restriction.md

symptom: NPS MFA extension auth fails. Multiple certificates exist locally from repeated configuration script runs without proper parameter changes.
rootCause: First run generated cert locally but failed to upload to AAD. Subsequent runs created additional certs without cleaning old ones, causing cert confusion.
solution: Delete excess local certs, ensure only one valid cert matching AAD. Verify AAD cert via Connect-MgGraph and Get-MgServicePrincipal for appid 981f26a1-7f43-403b-a875-f8b09b8cd720.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/MFA On-prem (not MFA server)/MFA extension with NPS server/2601260040000488 Case Study - NPS MFA.md

symptom: NPS MFA extension fails to find user in AAD. On-prem AD UPN format differs from AAD UPN.
rootCause: NPS extension defaults to on-premises AD UPN for AAD user lookup. When on-prem UPN (e.g. user@domain.local) differs from AAD UPN, lookup fails.
solution: Configure registry LDAP_ALTERNATE_LOGINID_ATTRIBUTE to specify AD attribute matching AAD UPN (typically mail). Restart NPS service. Ref: howto-mfa-nps-extension-advanced.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/MFA On-prem (not MFA server)/MFA extension with NPS server/2601260040000488 Case Study - NPS MFA.md

symptom: NPS MFA triggers SMS verification but VPN client cannot input verification code. NPS log: Challenge requested in Authentication Ext for User.
rootCause: Azure MFA defaults to SMS. VPN clients using RADIUS cannot handle challenge-response for SMS/OTP input. After number matching update, default behavior changed.
solution: (1) Change user default MFA method to Authenticator push in AAD portal. (2) Configure NPS registry OVERRIDE_NUMBER_MATCHING_WITH_OTP=FALSE for legacy Approve/Deny push. (3) Restart NPS service.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/MFA On-prem (not MFA server)/MFA extension with NPS server/2601260040000488 Case Study - NPS MFA.md

symptom: NPS primary auth fails before MFA extension is invoked. Event 6273 in Security log. VPN client cannot connect despite NPS MFA extension installed.
rootCause: VPN client auth method does not match NPS network policy auth method. Mismatch causes NPS layer-1 primary auth failure before MFA extension (layer-2) is reached.
solution: (1) Enable NPS audit: auditpol /set /subcategory:Network Policy Server /success:enable /failure:enable. (2) Check event 6273 for details. (3) Temporarily remove MFA DLL registry (AuthorizationDLLs/ExtensionDLLs under authsrv\parameters) to isolate. (4) Configure matching auth (e.g. PEAP-MSCHAPv2) on both NPS and VPN client.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/MFA On-prem (not MFA server)/MFA extension with NPS server/2601260040000488 Case Study - NPS MFA.md

symptom: Enabling Certificate-Based Authentication (CBA) in Azure China (21Vianet) fails when using Microsoft Graph v1.0 API endpoint. No UI available for CBA configuration in Mooncake portal.
rootCause: 21Vianet Entra ID CBA configuration requires the beta API endpoint (microsoftgraph.chinacloudapi.cn/beta/), not the standard v1.0 endpoint. The CBA management UI is not yet available in Mooncake environment.
solution: Use Microsoft Graph beta API with PowerShell to enable CBA: Connect-MgGraph -Environment China, then Update-MgBetaPolicyAuthenticationMethodPolicyAuthenticationMethodConfiguration with X509Certificate config. Set authenticationModeConfiguration to x509CertificateMultiFactor for MFA-equivalent CBA. Upload root/intermediate CA certs to PKI Trust Store via Graph API.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/Multi-factor Auth/Authentication methods/Certificate based auth.md

symptom: Bulk enable MFA via MFA portal for more than 50 users partially fails — some users remain unenabled but the portal reports all operations successful.
rootCause: MFA portal bulk enable operation has a known limitation when processing large batches (>50 users), silently failing to enable MFA for some users without reporting errors.
solution: Use PowerShell script with Set-MsolUser -StrongAuthenticationRequirements to bulk enable MFA. After execution, verify each user's status with Get-MsolUser checking StrongAuthenticationRequirements.State. Export results to CSV and retry users with empty State values.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/Multi-factor Auth/Case study/MFA-[case sharing]  bulk MFA enable via PowerShell.md

symptom: Unable to configure 'Verification options' under Service settings of per-user MFA in Azure portal. The settings page fails to save changes.
rootCause: The rememberMfaOnTrustedDevice.allowedNumberOfDays field has an invalid value (0 or null) in the mfaServicePolicy, preventing the verification options configuration from being saved.
solution: Submit a PATCH request to the mfaServicePolicy endpoint with rememberMfaOnTrustedDevice.allowedNumberOfDays set to a value between 1 and 90. Use F12/Insomnia to send the PATCH with the full policy body including mfaPolicySettings and trustedIps.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/Multi-factor Auth/Case study/[AAD] Unable to configure _Verification options_.md

symptom: Customer concerned that MFA phone calls/SMS for 21Vianet (Mooncake) M365 services originate from international numbers outside China mainland, raising data compliance and PIPL cross-border transfer concerns.
rootCause: 21Vianet uses centralized global telecom services to deliver MFA SMS/voice calls. End users' phone numbers are transferred to global telecom service providers outside mainland China during MFA authentication.
solution: Share CELA official response: 21Vianet has obtained prior authorization under customer agreement DPA for cross-border transfers. The DPA incorporates PIPL Article 21 entrusted processing requirements. 21Vianet commits to conducting personal information protection impact assessments and adopting appropriate transfer mechanisms. No current non-compliance under effective PRC law. If PRC law changes, 21Vianet will revisit and take measures. Mark communication as internal-only reference.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/Multi-factor Auth/Mooncake MFA with international caller ID.md

symptom: Cannot query MFA logs from Jarvis SASPerRequestEvent / SASCommonEvent tables for Mooncake. Data not available in these Jarvis tables since Dec 2025.
rootCause: Jarvis SAS tables (SASPerRequestEvent, SASCommonEvent) were deprecated for MFA log queries in Mooncake as of Dec 2025.
solution: Use Kusto queries instead: cluster('idsharedmcsha.chinaeast2.kusto.chinacloudapi.cn').database('idmfacne'). SAS logs: AllSASCommonEvents table (filter by env_time, userObjectId/TrackingID). CAPP logs: AllCappLogEvents table (filter by env_time, userObjectId, smsMessageId). TrackingID = ESTS correlation ID.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/Multi-factor Auth/Kusto.md

solution: 1) Confirm MFA works for other apps (OWA). 2) Check ASC if user has phone auth method registered. 3) Register phone at https://mysignins.windowsazure.cn/security-info. 4) Admin check per-user MFA config to ensure phone method allowed.

symptom: AADJ device cannot SSO to on-premise share folder with WHFB PIN. Works with username+password. Error: cannot find DC or domain not available.
rootCause: WHFB key trust or certificate trust not configured for hybrid deployment. SSO with PIN requires: WS2016+ DC, Kerberos certs on DCs, PKI with HTTP CRL, root cert on devices.
solution: Configure WHFB key trust or cert trust. Requirements: 1) WS2016+ DC, 2) Kerberos certs with HTTP CRL on DCs, 3) Root cert deployed to devices. Verify with netmon trace.

symptom: Kerberos auth fails with KDC_ERR_CLIENT_NAME_MISMATCH when AADJ user accesses on-prem share with WHFB PIN. KDC ETL shows user not found, LDAP query on msDS-KeyCredentialLink returns empty.
rootCause: msDS-KeyCredentialLink attribute empty in on-prem AD. Should be written back by AAD Connect. Common causes: Connect account lacks write permission or sync rule issue.
solution: 1) Verify msDS-KeyCredentialLink exists for user in on-prem AD. 2) If empty, grant Connect account permission: add to Key Admins group or run dsacls. 3) Check AADConnect sync rules and operations.

symptom: AADJ device cannot SSO to on-prem share folder after initial join. Only works after rebooting (sometimes multiple reboots). Netmon shows no DC locator attempt.
rootCause: By design, AADJ device needs reboot after initial setup to cache domain info (DNS domain, NetBIOS, SAM account name from AAD Connect). Without reboot, no DC locator, falls back to NTLM.
solution: Reboot the AADJ device after initial join. By-design behavior. After reboot, device performs DC locator via DNS, obtains Kerberos TGT for SSO.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/Windows Hello for business/Case study/Not able to SSO to on-premise Share folder from AA.md

symptom: msDS-KeyCredentialLink in on-prem AD keeps being written back by AAD Connect sync after manual deletion. Customer cannot access original Windows devices to remove WHfB enrollment.

solution: Delete source in Azure AD: 1) Verify WHfB via browser F12 checking windowsHelloForBusinessAuthenticationMethod. 2) DELETE via Graph API: https://microsoftgraph.chinacloudapi.cn/beta/users/<USERID>/authentication/windowsHelloForBusinessMethods/<methodID>. Works in 21Vianet despite docs saying unsupported.

symptom: AAD Connect Directory Extension 配置时无法选择 on-prem AD 中某些扩展属性类型（如 Case insensitive string），导致自定义属性无法从 on-prem 同步到 AAD

symptom: AAD Connect Device Writeback 部分设备无法同步回 on-prem AD，报错 'Error in evaluation of expression' 涉及 stkDeviceKey 和 'This implementation is not part of the Windows Platform FIPS validated cryptographic algorithms'
rootCause: .NET 4.6.2 之前版本的 SHA256.Create() 默认使用 SHA256Managed（不兼容 FIPS），导致 device key 计算失败。Sync Rule 'In from AAD - Device Common' 中的表达式依赖 SHA256
solution: 升级 .NET Framework 到 4.6.2 或更高版本。该版本将 SHA256.Create() 改为使用 SHA256Cng（FIPS 兼容）
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Sync/AAD Connect/Case study/[Device writeback] Azure _ Intune _ write back iss.md

symptom: AD 中的 Group 和 User 已通过 AAD Connect 同步到云端，但用户的 Primary Group 的 group membership 未同步
rootCause: AAD Connect 不查询 PrimaryGroupID 属性来构建 group membership（设计限制）。AAD Connect 仅基于 memberOf 属性同步，而 Primary Group 不在 memberOf 列表中
solution: 这是 AAD Connect 的 by design 行为。用户的 Primary Group 成员关系不会被同步。参考 KB4014115。如需在云端体现该成员关系，需在 on-prem AD 中将 Primary Group 改为 Domain Users 以外的组或调整应用逻辑
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Sync/AAD Connect/Case study/[Group sync] Azure AD Connect excludes synchronize.md

symptom: AAD Connect upgrade wizard shows 'The provided user is not an enterprise admin' even though the account is in Enterprise Admins group of root domain. Product bug in AD DS credential page during upgrade (ICM 81048194).
rootCause: Product bug in AADC AD DS credential page during upgrade: the membership lookup fails to find the Enterprise Admin SID for certain accounts in multi-domain forests.
solution: Use the root domain admin account (e.g. ROOTDOMAIN\admin) instead of child domain EA account. PG workaround of adding EA as local admin on primary ADFS server may not work in all environments. Fixed in later AADC releases.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Sync/AAD Connect/Case study/[Installation] AAD connect upgrading issue.md

symptom: AAD Connect installation fails with 'user_realm_discovery_failed' error during Azure AD credential validation.
rootCause: Proxy server or proxy settings on the AAD Connect server block outbound connections to Azure AD endpoints. Even if admin claims no proxy, IE LAN settings may have 'Automatically detect settings' enabled or other proxy configurations under the service account context.
solution: Configure proxy settings per AAD Connect prerequisites doc. Log on to AAD Connect server with the service account, open IE > LAN settings, check/uncheck 'Automatically detect settings'. Verify direct Internet connectivity to Azure AD endpoints. Capture network trace to confirm blocked traffic.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Sync/AAD Connect/Case study/[Installation] failed to install AAD connect with.md

symptom: After deploying a new Entra ID Connect server, objects outside the explicitly selected OUs are synchronized to Entra ID, causing large-scale unintended sync. OU filtering appears ineffective.
rootCause: Product bug (ICM 21000000942261): When no domain/OU is selected during initial AADC config, domain root DN is written into BOTH ContainerInclusionList and ContainerExclusionList. When OUs are later selected, domain root DN remains in inclusion list. Sync engine prioritizes inclusion, so entire domain syncs.
solution: Workaround: 1) Reconfigure OU filtering to Mode A - explicitly select the domain, then uncheck unwanted OUs. This rewrites inclusion/exclusion lists correctly. 2) After corrected, safely switch back to Mode B (OU selection without domain). Verify with Get-ADSyncConnector PowerShell checking ContainerInclusionList/ContainerExclusionList.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Sync/AAD Connect/Case study/[OU filtering] A bug in OU filtering causing large.md

symptom: AAD Connect wizard fails at 'Connect Directory' with 'failed-authentication' error (0x31) for a specific forest while other forests connect fine. OU filtering page crashes with NullReferenceException.
rootCause: DNS zone dynamic updates set to 'None', preventing DC SRV records from registering in DNS. DNS SRV for the forest PDC resolved to a non-DC IP. LDAP authentication failed because wrong server was contacted. SPN format also abnormal (ldap/domain instead of ldap/dc-name/domain).
solution: 1) Check DNS SRV records: nslookup -type=SRV _ldap._tcp.dc._msdcs.<domain>. 2) Set DNS zone 'Dynamic updates' to 'Secure only'. 3) Restart netlogon service to re-register SRV records. 4) Verify LDAP bind with LDP.exe using the service account. 5) Retry AAD Connect wizard.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Sync/AAD Connect/Case study/[OU filtering] AD无法与AAD Connect同步 TrackingID#21122.md

symptom: Admin can reset password from O365 admin portal but fails from Azure Portal with password policy violation. Password writeback applies on-prem password policy inconsistently depending on reset method.
rootCause: By design: Azure Portal password reset triggers password writeback (applies on-prem policy). O365 portal and PowerShell (Set-MsolUserPassword, Set-AzureADUserPassword) do NOT trigger writeback - they only apply cloud password policy.
solution: This is by design. Educate customer: 1) Azure portal reset triggers writeback, on-prem policy applies. 2) O365 portal/PowerShell reset is cloud-only, cloud policy applies, password not written back. 3) End users can only trigger writeback via self-service password reset portal. 4) Using PowerShell may create 2 sets of passwords (cloud vs on-prem).
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Sync/AAD Connect/Case study/[Password writeback] Azure and O365 password reset.md

symptom: Password writeback stops working. Users cannot reset passwords via self-service portal. Support code shown on password reset portal may not appear in Application event log on AAD Connect server.
rootCause: Internal bad state of password writeback configuration, proxy/connectivity issues, or unsupported OS.
solution:
1) Get support code from password reset portal. 2) Search Application event log on AAD Connect server. 3) If not found, check prerequisites. 4) Reset writeback config:
$conn = Get-ADSyncConnector -name '<tenant>.onmicrosoft.com - AAD'
Set-ADSyncAADPasswordResetConfiguration -Connector $conn.Name -Enable $true
Get-ADSyncAADPasswordResetConfiguration -Connector $conn.Name
5) Check proxy/connectivity to Azure endpoints.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Sync/AAD Connect/Case study/[Password writeback] Password Writeback is not wor.md

symptom: AADC upgrade fails with 'Could not connect to primary ADFS server' because primary ADFS server changed but AADC still references old server
rootCause: AADC stores initial ADFS primary server info in PersistedState.xml (Base64 encoded). Server change does not auto-update this config
solution: Edit %ProgramData%\AADConnect\PersistedState.xml, find IAdfsContext.TargetAdfsServers, decode old Base64 server name, encode new server FQDN to Base64, replace, then retry upgrade
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Sync/AAD Connect/Case study/[Installation] AAD connect upgrading issue.md

symptom: Multiple failures (AADC Health Agent install, Connect-MsolService, AADC wizard Azure AD connection) with NET.WebException / 'Could not create SSL/TLS secure channel'
rootCause: Server missing .NET Framework TLS 1.2 strong crypto (SchUseStrongCrypto), cannot communicate with Azure AD endpoints that deprecated TLS 1.0/1.1
solution: Set registry: Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\.NetFramework\v4.0.30319' -Name 'SchUseStrongCrypto' -Value '1' -Type DWord; also set Wow6432Node path
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Sync/AAD Connect/Case study/[Installation] Enabled strong cryptography for TLS.md

symptom: AADC upgrade from 1.1.819.0 to 1.1.882.0 fails with IndexOutOfRangeException at BuildMsiArguments
rootCause: AADC sync service account in registry ObjectName uses UPN format (user@domain.com) instead of Domain\User. Upgrade code splits on backslash to get domain/user, UPN format causes array index error
solution: In Services.msc, change Microsoft Azure AD Sync logon account back to Domain\Username format, restart service, retry upgrade. Bug 510761 filed with PG
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Sync/AAD Connect/Case study/[Installation] Upgrading to 1.1.882.0 Error Failed.md

symptom: Password writeback suddenly fails (was working before), Application event log shows 'make sure the target object is in scope of password sync rule', reinstalling AADC fixes it
rootCause: Password writeback flows via sync rule 'Out to AAD - User Join' with 'Enable Password Sync' checked. If unchecked (even when Password Sync not enabled in AADC config), password writeback fails
solution: In Synchronization Rules Editor, check 'Out to AAD - User Join' sync rule, ensure 'Enable Password Sync' is checked. Run Full Sync after. No need to reinstall AADC
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Sync/AAD Connect/Case study/[Password writeback] Password writeback with sync.md

symptom: AAD Connect export slow with 503 Server Unavailable errors. Application log shows ServerDownException and EndpointNotFoundException for adminwebservice.partner.microsoftonline.cn/ProvisioningService.svc.
rootCause: Two network issues: (1) Proxy not allowing *.partner.microsoftonline.cn traffic; (2) WAN backbone (msn.net) routing loop causing connectivity failure to AAD provisioning endpoint.
solution: 1. Open *.partner.microsoftonline.cn on proxy; 2. Fix backbone routing loop. Verify with Invoke-WebRequest -Uri https://adminwebservice.partner.microsoftonline.cn/ProvisioningService.svc
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Sync/AAD Connect/Case study/[Performance]AADConnect Export Slow.md

symptom: AAD Connect export extremely slow with delays between export iterations (e.g. Iteration 70 to 71 takes ~2 hours). No 503 errors but export batches take excessively long.
rootCause: AAD Connect using Named Pipes protocol to connect to external SQL server, and SQL Server compatibility level set to 2008 instead of 2016.
solution: 1. Disable Named Pipes in SQL Server configuration (3 settings); 2. Change SQL 2016 compatibility level from 2008 to 2016; 3. Reboot SQL server or restart DB services.

symptom: AAD Connect Delta Synchronization takes ~3 hours even with no object updates. Huge number of disconnectors visible in connector space.
rootCause: DEV AAD Connect server configured with PROD AAD tenant. Objects from PROD AAD not linkable to DEV on-prem AD creating massive disconnectors. TCP ZeroWindow observed. Unsupported multi-sync-server topology.
solution: Correct AAD Connect config to point to the correct AAD tenant. Troubleshooting: check disconnector count, verify topology, capture network trace (netsh trace) for TCP ZeroWindow.

rootCause: SyncCycleEnabled is set to False in the ADSync scheduler configuration.

symptom: LargeObject error during AAD Connect export: The provisioned object is too large. Other attribute changes for the same object also blocked.
rootCause: Azure AD enforces max 15 certificate values on userCertificate attribute. Objects with >15 values trigger LargeObject error.
solution: Options: 1. Upgrade AADC to 1.1.524.0+ (OOB rules skip >15 certs); 2. Outbound sync rule exporting NULL for >15 certs; 3. Remove expired certs from on-prem AD; 4. Exclude userCertificate (not recommended).

rootCause: MFA was enabled for the AAD Connect sync service account, blocking non-interactive authentication.
solution: Disable MFA for the synchronization service account in Azure AD / Entra ID portal.

symptom: SMTP proxy address with unverified domain suffix not visible in O365 portal for migrated mailbox users. Address in shadowProxyAddresses but not in proxyAddresses.
rootCause: ProxyCal filters unverified domain addresses when user has Exchange license and MSExchRemoteRecipientType is not NULL (set to 4 after mailbox migration).
solution: Workaround: Delete MSExchRemoteRecipientType value in on-prem AD using ADSIEDIT. Permanent fix: Verify the domain in Azure AD.

symptom: On-prem AD account with expired accountExpires attribute remains enabled in Azure AD. accountExpires not synced to cloud.
rootCause: AAD Connect does not sync accountExpires. Only UserAccountControl (enabled/disabled) synced via In from AD - User Common rule. Expired account still enabled until explicitly disabled.
solution: Scheduled PowerShell script: Search-ADAccount -AccountExpired to find, Disable-ADAccount to disable. AAD Connect then syncs disabled state via UserAccountControl.

symptom: SMTP proxy address synced to shadowProxyAddresses but not in final proxyAddresses. ProxyCal discards the value silently.
rootCause: Hidden/invisible bad characters in SMTP proxy address in on-prem AD (after @ and at end). Invisible in AD tools but visible in CMD. ProxyCal rejects malformed address.
solution: 1. Paste SMTP value into CMD to reveal hidden chars; 2. Re-enter value cleanly in on-prem AD; 3. Run Start-ADSyncSyncCycle for delta sync.

rootCause: EXO BackSync writes mail@tenant.onmicrosoft.com to AAD mail attribute, overriding shadow mail from AAD Connect sync.
solution: Manually re-sync: 1. Change on-prem primary SMTP to onmicrosoft.com, add real domain as secondary; 2. Delta sync; 3. Remove onmicrosoft entry; 4. Restore primary SMTP; 5. Delta sync again.

symptom: Office client (OneNote/Outlook) shows FixMe button and yellow triangle in MeControl. After clicking FixMe, CC B2B (cross-cloud B2B) error appears. User has accounts on both public cloud and 21V cloud.
rootCause: Office client CredAccessor constructed without specific account, tries both global and Gallatin accounts. When CA requires re-auth, both accounts fail silently, triggering FixMe. FixMe attempts sign-in using Gallatin login_hint against public Entra ID endpoint, triggering CC B2B flow that fails because accounts are not invited cross-cloud.
solution: Workaround: Sign out the Gallatin/21V account so only one cloud account is active. Or ask admin to relax CA policy. Long-term fix requires code change in Office identity client (MeControl owned by Office Lifecycle Programmable Surfaces/FIO team).

symptom: Newer Android devices cannot login Outlook when Intune Company Portal installed. White screen after entering username. Works if Company Portal uninstalled. Older devices and iOS not affected.
rootCause: With Intune, Outlook uses Company Portal broker via WebView for SSO. ADFS server/NLB only returns leaf SSL certificate without intermediate certificates. Android WebView cannot auto-complete certificate chains unlike Chrome/Edge, causing SSL_UNTRUSTED error. Newer Android versions enforce stricter SSL chain validation.
solution: Configure ADFS server or front-end NLB to return full certificate chain including intermediate certificates. Verify with: openssl s_client -connect adfs.domain.com:443 -showcerts
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/M365 Identity IDO/## Case study/Outlook cannot sign when Intune managed.md

symptom: Outlook Mobile in 21V cloud prompts error when attaching files after migrating from third-party MDM to Intune. AADSTS500011 InvalidResourceServicePrincipalNotFound for https://substrate.office.com.
rootCause: Outlook Mobile sends token request for https://substrate.office.com (public cloud resource) instead of https://substrate.partner.outlook.cn (21V cloud resource). SSO enablement via Intune/broker triggers the issue.
solution: Product bug in Outlook Mobile. O365 OM team owns the fix to use correct 21V resource endpoint (substrate.partner.outlook.cn) when sending requests in Gallatin environment.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/M365 Identity IDO/## Case study/Outlook mobile prompt error, resource named https_.md

symptom: 21v Teams 登录时无限循环要求用户注册 MFA，即使 admin 已禁用 per-user MFA 或未配置 Conditional Access。
rootCause: 中国网络安全法第 24 条要求实名认证。21v Teams 强制要求终端用户注册手机号码作为 MFA 验证方式，此要求无法通过 per-user MFA (Disabled)、Security Defaults 或 CA Policy 绕过。
solution: 1) 确认 MFA 在其他应用 (如 OWA partner.outlook.cn) 是否正常。2) 如仅 Teams 有问题，在 ASC 查看用户是否已注册 phone auth method。3) 未注册则让用户访问 https://mysignins.windowsazure.cn/security-info 注册手机号。4) 若无法注册，admin 检查 per-user MFA 配置页确认 phone method 已允许。5) 若仍有问题，按常规 MFA 排查收集 Authenticator/Windows event/Teams logs。
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/M365 Identity IDO/## Common operation_TSG/MFA limitation with 21v Teams.md

symptom: Office 客户端反复弹出 WAM 认证提示 (0xCAA10001 Need user interaction)，用户频繁被要求重新登录。
rootCause: WAM 被调用时未传入 web account (webAccountCount=0)，只传了 LoginHint。当 LoginHint 的 UPN 与 Windows 已登录域用户不一致时，WAM 返回 0xCAA10001 (ERROR_ADAL_NEED_CREDENTIAL) 触发认证提示。首次认证成功后 WAM 会缓存 RT 和 web account，后续带 web account 调用可正常静默获取 token。
solution: 1) 分析 WAM 日志 (Event ID 0x4AA50119) 确认 webAccountCount 和 LoginHint。2) 如果是首次登录触发的提示属正常行为。3) 如果反复出现，检查 Office 是否正确传递 web account 信息。4) 使用 WAM diagnostics tool (aka.ms/wamhot) 清理 token cache 后重新登录。5) 确认 LoginHint UPN 是否与期望用户一致。
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/M365 Identity IDO/## Common operation_TSG/Sample WAM log analysis.md

rootCause: Win10 1709 Cloud AP Plugin uses UPN from logon cache (HKLM\\SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\IdentityStore\\LogonCache) which contained incorrect onmicrosoft.com suffix instead of federated domain. Bug fixed in Win10 1803

symptom: SSO fails with SAML assertion too old error. Application rejects AuthnInstant timestamp as stale (e.g. months old) even though SAML token Conditions NotBefore/NotOnOrAfter are current.
rootCause: Mismatch between Azure AD SSO session token lifetime and application Max Authentication Age setting. Persistent tokens have 180-day lifetime; nonpersistent 24 hours. Application was configured with shorter Max Auth Age (e.g. 92 days) than Azure AD token lifetime.
solution: Align application Max Authentication Age with Azure AD token lifetime. For persistent tokens: set app Max Auth to >= 181 days. Review Azure AD configurable token lifetime policies for reference.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/Device Registration/Case study/SSO-[case sharing] What is the maximum length of a.md

symptom: AADSTS50107: Requested federation realm object does not exist. Error occurs after setting up Hybrid Azure AD with ADFS claim rules for device registration.
rootCause: Claim rule script run with  =  when only one domain is federated. This creates an issuerID using regexreplace that produces incorrect issuer URI (e.g. domain.com/adfs/services/trust instead of sso.domain.com/adfs/services/trust).
solution: Run the claim rules script with  = . Verify correct issuer URI via Get-MsolDomainFederationSettings -DomainName <domain>. Only use  when multiple domains are actually federated.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/Device Registration/Case study/SSO-[case sharing]SSO not working for Hybrid AAD.md

symptom: AAD SAML SSO application uses tenant-level certificate instead of application-level certificate. SSO breaks periodically (monthly) because tenant-level cert rolls over frequently. Certificate issuer shows accounts.accesscontrol.windows.net instead of Microsoft Azure Federated SSO Certificate.
rootCause: UseCustomTokenSigningKey attribute is missing for the application in backend. Likely caused by creating the application from old Azure portal. Without this attribute, federation metadata only contains tenant-level certificates.
solution: In Azure portal: go to application Single sign-on page → Create new certificate → Mark new certificate active → Save. Verify in federation metadata that new cert issuer is Microsoft Azure Federated SSO Certificate. Update the new certificate in the SP (application) side.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/Device Registration/Case study/SSO-[sharing]AAD SAML SSO application use tenant l.md

symptom: Windows 10 Azure AD join (workplace join) fails with error code 80180026. Device successfully joins Azure AD then immediately initiates un-join (DEVICE_UNJOIN) within seconds. Event log shows AAD Cloud AP plugin error 0xC000023C and HTTP 400 on sidtoname endpoint.
rootCause: MDM auto-enroll conflicts with Azure AD workplace join. Device had previously enrolled via MDM (Intune), and MDM auto-enroll was still enabled, causing the immediate unjoin after successful join.
solution: Uninstall Intune manage MSI from the PC. Disable MDM auto-enroll policy. Retry Azure AD join after both steps are completed.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/Device Registration/Case study/Windows 10 Azure AD join failure at error code 801.md

symptom: Applications using pure ADAL (non-WAM) library do not benefit from PRT SSO. MFA prompts repeat across applications even though MFA was already completed in another app.
rootCause: PRT is only created/updated by WAM (broker) and Cloud AP plugin flows. Pure ADAL library bypasses WAM, so MFA claim is not stored in PRT. Other apps using WAM cannot see the MFA satisfaction.
solution: Migrate applications from ADAL to MSAL with broker (WAM) enabled. Ensure apps use WAM-aware authentication to share PRT and MFA claims across all apps on the device.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/Device Registration/Concepts/How SSO works with WAM.md

symptom: SSO stops working for desktop apps after user only uses browser for extended period. PRT becomes stale, apps prompt for credentials again.
rootCause: Browser SSO (IE/Edge/Chrome extension) consumes PRT to generate SSO cookie, but does NOT update/renew PRT. PRT is only renewed during Windows logon or WAM-based token acquisition. Extended browser-only usage causes PRT to age without renewal.
solution: Ensure at least one WAM-based app acquires token periodically (triggers PRT renewal every 4 hours). If issue persists, lock/unlock workstation to trigger Cloud AP PRT renewal via Windows logon flow.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/Device Registration/Concepts/How SSO works with WAM.md

symptom: SAML SSO fails: application rejects AuthnStatement as too old. SAML response AuthnInstant timestamp is months old despite valid token NotBefore/NotOnOrAfter window. Error: 'authentication statement is too old to be used'.
rootCause: Mismatch between Azure AD SSO session token lifetime (persistent=180d, nonpersistent=24h) and application Max Authentication Age setting. Azure AD reuses AuthnInstant from original authentication, but application enforces stricter max age (e.g., 92 days).
solution: Align application Max Authentication Age with Azure AD token lifetime. E.g., change Jive Max Authentication from 92 days to 181 days (> AAD persistent token 180d). Ref: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory/active-directory-configurable-token-lifetimes
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/Device Registration/Case study/SSO-[case sharing] What is the maximum length of a.md

symptom: SAML SSO to third-party application fails. Azure AD shows permission error from unexpected tenant. Fiddler trace reveals SAML request has incorrect identifier (Entity ID) URL.
rootCause: SP (e.g., Replicon) misconfigured the SAML SSO endpoint - the Identifier (Entity ID) URL was missing a path segment (e.g., '/saml2/'), causing it to point to wrong tenant or endpoint.
solution: Compare the Identifier URL in Azure AD SAML SSO configuration with the expected SP endpoint. Fix the missing path segment. Use Fiddler trace and 'Test SAML SSO' button in Azure Portal to verify the SAML request flow.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/Device Registration/Case study/SSO-[case sharing] _ Can't login using SSO in Repl.md

symptom: AADSTS50107: Requested federation realm object 'http://{domain}/adfs/services/trust/' does not exist. Occurs after running Hybrid Azure AD join claim rules script.
rootCause: Claim rules script was run with $multipleVerifiedDomainNames=$true, but tenant has only one federated domain among multiple verified domains. The regex-based IssuerID rule rewrites issuer URI to http://{domain}/adfs/services/trust/ which does not match the actual federation issuer URI (e.g., http://sso.{domain}/adfs/services/trust/).
solution: Run the Hybrid AAD join claim rules script with $multipleVerifiedDomainNames=$false when only one domain is federated. Verify actual issuer URI with Get-MsolDomainFederationSettings -DomainName <domain>. Ref: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory/connect/active-directory-aadconnect-multiple-domains
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/Device Registration/Case study/SSO-[case sharing]SSO not working for Hybrid AAD.md

symptom: AAD SAML SSO application periodically breaks (e.g., monthly). SAML signing certificate uses tenant-level certificate (issuer: accounts.accesscontrol.windows.net) instead of application-level certificate (issuer: Microsoft Azure Federated SSO Certificate). Tenant-level cert rolls over frequently.
rootCause: Application backend is missing UseCustomTokenSigningKey attribute, likely because app was created from old Azure portal. Without this attribute, federation metadata only contains tenant-level certificates.
solution: 1) Azure Portal > Enterprise Apps > Single sign-on > Create new certificate. 2) Mark new certificate active and Save. 3) Verify new cert in app-specific metadata URL. 4) Confirm issuer is 'Microsoft Azure Federated SSO Certificate'. 5) Upload new cert to SP (e.g., SAP).

rootCause: Device was previously MDM enrolled (Intune). MDM auto-enroll is enabled and conflicts with Azure AD join (workplace join) process. The MDM enrollment triggers the un-join operation after initial AAD join succeeds.
solution: 1) Uninstall Intune manage MSI from the PC. 2) Disable MDM auto-enroll. 3) Retry Azure AD join.

symptom: Seamless SSO fails in IE. Fiddler shows 'Frame returned code 105 with message IFrame: Running in an un-trusted zone. Integrated Windows Authentication failed'. IE does not use Kerberos SSO or prompt for WIA.
rootCause: IE setting 'Allow status bar updates via script' is disabled under Internet Options > Local Intranet > Custom Level. Seamless SSO requires this setting to check/change status bar during SSO IFrame flow.
solution: Enable 'Allow status bar updates via script' in IE: Internet Options > Security > Local Intranet > Custom Level > Scripting > 'Allow status bar updates via script' > Enable.

symptom: AADJ device joined via OOBE does not allow cached offline logon immediately after OOBE completes. After disconnecting from network, user is required to connect to network to complete authentication.
rootCause: By design. Cached logon is an optimization feature, not behavioral. After OOBE, user must complete one full logon cycle (log off then log on) before cached credential is stored for offline use.
solution: 1) Join machine to AAD via OOBE. 2) Log off the user. 3) Log on the user (this caches the credential). 4) Disconnect from network. 5) Log on same user - cached logon should work. Do not rely on cached logon working immediately after OOBE without the extra logon cycle.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Authentication/Device Registration/Feature_specific configuration/Cached Credential behavior in AADJ and OOBE.md

symptom: AAD Connect v1.x stops syncing after Microsoft decommissioned v1 connectivity. Sync breaks without clear error for tenants not yet upgraded.
rootCause: Microsoft deprecated AAD Connect v1.x (since Aug 2022) and began blocking v1.x connections to Admin Web Service (AWS/Azure AD) in rolling batches starting 2023/10/1. By Jan 2024 ~27% of tenants blocked, continuing rollout.
solution: Upgrade to AAD Connect v2.x immediately. The decommission is rolling - some tenants may still work temporarily but will eventually be blocked. No fix for v1 - upgrade is the only path.

symptom: After AAD Connect upgrade, federated domain authentication fails. IssuerID in ADFS claim rule is corrupted - e.g. 'technodyne.co.uk' issuerID becomes 'http://co.uk/adfs/services/trust/' instead of 'http://technodyne.co.uk/adfs/services/trust/'.
rootCause: AAD Connect upgrade process replaces the 'Issue the IssuerID when it is not a computer account' ADFS claim rule with a buggy regex: regexreplace(c1.Value, "^((.*)([.|@]))?(?<domain>[^.]*[.].*)$", ...) which strips subdomain from two-part TLD domains like .co.uk.
solution: Manually fix the ADFS claim rule. Replace the corrupted regex with: regexreplace(c1.Value, ".+@(?<domain>.+)", "http://${domain}/adfs/services/trust/"). This is a known AADC bug - verify correct issuerID via Get-MsolDomainFederationSettings.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Sync/AAD Connect/Case study/[ADFS config] Azure AD connect bug.md

symptom: General AAD Connect sync issues - objects not syncing to Azure AD as expected.
rootCause: Common causes: object not in synced OU, outdated AADC version, object pending in connector space after OU changes without full sync.
solution: General troubleshooting steps: 1) Check if object is in the synced OU. 2) Update AADC to latest version. 3) Use Preview for specific object in Metaverse. 4) If object not in Metaverse, check Connector Space by searching RDN='CN=...'. 5) After OU changes, run Full Sync. Logs: Application event log on AADC server + Fiddler on client if user-side symptom.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Sync/AAD Connect/Case study/AAD_Sync General Troubleshooting.md

symptom: AAD Connect v1.x stops syncing after retirement. Connection to Admin Web Service (AWS) blocked in rolling batches starting Oct 1, 2023. Sync breaks without warning for affected tenants in Mooncake.
rootCause: Microsoft deprecated AAD Connect v1.x (retired Aug 31, 2022) due to SQL Server 2012 EOL. Engineering blocks tenants in batches (~27% blocked by Dec 2023). V1.x cannot connect to Azure AD after block.
solution: Upgrade to AAD Connect v2.x. Note: v2 requires Windows Server 2016+; cannot install on 2008R2/2012/2012R2 — must upgrade OS first or build new server. Consider Azure AD Cloud Sync as alternative. Include upgrade notice in all sync case closing emails.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Sync/AAD Connect/Case study/AADConnect v1 retirement.md

symptom: Need to identify tenants still using AAD Connect v1.x in Mooncake to confirm whether retirement blocking applies to a specific tenant.
rootCause: AAD Connect v1.x retirement is rolling; some tenants still using v1 and not yet blocked. Need KQL on msodsmooncake cluster to check AWS logs.
solution: Query msodsmooncake.chinanorth2.kusto: Global('IfxUlsEvents') | where env_cloud_role=='adminwebservice' and tagId=='a51t' and message startswith '[Exit' | parse appId and headerDirSyncBuildNum | filter appId in ('1651564e-...', '6eb59a73-...') and headerDirSyncBuildNum startswith '1.' | summarize by contextId. This identifies v1 tenants still connecting.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Sync/AAD Connect/Case study/AADConnect v1 retirement/Case ID.md

symptom: After AAD Connect upgrade, ADFS federated SSO breaks. IssuerID claim rule regex changed, causing incorrect IssuerID for multi-part TLDs (e.g. technodyne.co.uk resolves to http://co.uk/adfs/services/trust/ instead of http://technodyne.co.uk/adfs/services/trust/).
rootCause: AAD Connect upgrade replaces the IssuerID claim rule regex from simple UPN-based pattern to broader regex (^((.*)([.|@]))?(?<domain>[^.]*[.].*)$) which incorrectly parses multi-part TLDs like .co.uk. This is a known AAD Connect bug.
solution: Manually revert the 'Issue the IssuerID when it is not a computer account' claim rule to use the simpler regex: c1:[Type=UPN] && c2:[Type=accounttype,Value=User] => issue(issuerid = regexreplace(c1.Value, '.+@(?<domain>.+)', 'http://${domain}/adfs/services/trust/')). Also check AAD Connect Health alerts for ADFS config issues and apply recommended fixes.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Sync/AAD Connect/Case study/[ADFS config] Azure AD connect bug.md

symptom: ADFS federation test fails in Mooncake (21Vianet) when ADFS site has not completed ISP (ICP) registration. TLS handshake broken for external connections.
rootCause: 21vbluecloud network breaks TLS for sites without ISP/ICP registration. Affects ADFS/WAP endpoints in Azure China.
solution: Keep test client in same vnet as ADFS. Complete ISP/ICP registration before external access. Use vnet peering for internal testing.

symptom: Cloud-only user cannot log on to VM joined to AADDS managed domain after enabling AADDS. Authentication fails.
rootCause: Cloud-only users need password hash synced to AADDS. Must change/reset password to trigger NTLM/Kerberos hash generation.
solution: Have cloud-only users change password after AADDS enabled to trigger hash sync. Wait ~20 min for sync. For synced users, ensure password hash sync enabled in AAD Connect.

symptom: Cannot create VM or enable AADDS inside Azure AD B2C tenant.
rootCause: B2C tenants are consumer identity (CIAM) only. No infrastructure resource support.
solution: B2C only supports user flows, custom policies, identity providers. Use standard Azure AD tenant for VM/AADDS. Link subscription to regular tenant.

symptom: Microsoft.Identity.AadConnect.Health.AadSync.Host.exe crashes (Application Error Event ID 1000). AAD Connect Health for Sync shows old or no information.
rootCause: Version mismatch between Azure AD Connect Synchronization Service and Azure AD Connect Health for Sync agent. E.g., Sync Service 1.1.647.0+ requires Health Agent 3.0.103.0 or 3.0.129.0, not 3.0.68.0.
solution: Method 1: Manually uninstall Health Agent and reinstall a compatible version per the compatibility table (Sync <=1.1.614.0 -> Health 3.0.127.0; Sync >=1.1.647.0 -> Health 3.0.129.0). Then register with Register-AzureADConnectHealthSyncAgent. Method 2: Upgrade AAD Connect to 1.1.649.0+ which upgrades both components.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Sync/AAD Connect/Feature not ready yet/[Connect health] Faulting application name_ Micros.md

symptom: AAD Connect Pass-Through Authentication (PTA) agent shows as Enabled but Inactive in portal. Event ID 12019 occurs every 30 seconds. Bootstrap requests fail with 403.
rootCause: On Windows Server 2012 R2, TLS hash algorithm SHA512 is disabled by default for TLS 1.2. The PTA connector cannot provide a client certificate during TLS handshake (missing cipher suites TLS_RSA_WITH_AES_256_GCM_SHA384 and TLS_RSA_WITH_AES_128_GCM_SHA256), causing the server to return 403 Forbidden.
solution: Install Windows update rollup KB2975719 (August 2014 update rollup for Windows 8.1/Server 2012 R2) on the PTA server to enable SHA512 for TLS 1.2.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Sync/AAD Connect/Feature not ready yet/[Pass-through Auth] AAD Connect Pass-Through Auth.md

symptom: AAD Connect AD connector shows no-start-ma status; cannot connect to on-premises Active Directory domain controller
rootCause: Domain controller locator process fails due to DNS misconfiguration, network connectivity issues, or Netlogon service problems preventing LDAP/RPC communication between AAD Connect server and AD DC
solution: 12-step diagnostic: 1) Check Event Viewer System+Directory Service logs 2) ipconfig /all 3) Ping IP+hostname+domain 4) netdiag /v 5) netdiag /fix 6) nltest /dsgetdc:domainname 7) NSLookup SRV records 8) ipconfig /registerdns or restart Netlogon 9) dcdiag /v 10) Ldp.exe test LDAP bind 11) nltest /dbflag:0x2000ffff for Netlogon debug 12) Network Monitor trace
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Sync/AAD Connect/TSG/Connectivity to AD.md

symptom: AAD Connect fails to sync through Kerberos-authenticated proxy; need to verify if AADC is sending correct Kerberos service ticket to proxy server
rootCause: Kerberos cipher mismatch or incorrect service account configuration in machine.config for .NET Framework proxy settings at C:\Windows\Microsoft.NET\Framework644.0.30319\Config\machine.config
solution: Capture simultaneous Wireshark traces on AADC server and proxy: 1) On AADC side filter Kerberos, find first HTTP frame, locate cipher (e.g. ea867e…) in Kerberos auth 2) Check TGS-REP above HTTP frame to verify CNameString matches AADC service account 3) On proxy side find same cipher in HTTP proxy-Authorization header. If ciphers match end-to-end, no MITM alteration. Also verify machine.config proxy connectivity settings per MS docs.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Sync/AAD Connect/TSG/Kerberos Auth proxy.md

symptom: Synced user has unwanted secondary proxyAddress with 4 random digits in initial domain suffix (e.g. alias1234@tenant.partner.onmschina.cn). Cannot remove via on-prem AD because initial domain proxyAddresses are system-managed in cloud.
rootCause: ProxyCalc generates UPN from MailNickname + Initial Domain when ShadowUPN and ShadowProxyAddresses primary SMTP are absent. If generated UPN conflicts with existing user, ProxyCalc auto-appends 4 random digits. This happens when cloud-only user exists before on-prem user is synced with same alias.
solution: Workaround using AutoReconcileProxyConflicts: 1) Pause AAD Connect sync scheduler (open Wizard) 2) Soft-delete the affected user (Remove-MgUser) 3) Create temp cloud-only user with UPN matching the unwanted proxyAddress 4) Edit temp user Contact Info to add the proxyAddress to remove 5) Restore original user with autoReconcileProxyConflict=true via Invoke-MgGraphRequest POST to /beta/directory/deletedItems/{id}/microsoft.graph.restore 6) Original user restored without conflicting proxyAddress 7) Delete temp user and resume sync. Note: extra secondary addresses are not harmful if workaround is too risky.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Sync/AAD Connect/TSG/Remove unwanted proxyAddress.md

symptom: Need to investigate server-side AAD object changes (e.g. user deletions, attribute modifications) that are not visible from client-side AAD Connect logs
rootCause: Client-side sync logs only show export operations; server-side processing (ProxyCalc, conflict resolution, policy enforcement) may alter or reject changes silently
solution: Use Jarvis AAD Change Tracking dashboard (https://portal.microsoftgeneva.com/s/E55DDB2E): 1) Set short time range 2) Add filter with target user objectId or tenantId 3) Find object change operations (e.g. deletion) 4) Use internalCorrelationID from result to filter and get full operation chain details across all related logs
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Sync/AAD Connect/Tools/Jarvis - AAD Change Tracking.md

symptom: Need to convert between ImmutableID (Base64) and on-premises AD objectGUID for AAD Connect sync troubleshooting
rootCause: AAD Connect uses ImmutableID (Base64-encoded objectGUID) as the source anchor to match on-prem AD objects with Azure AD objects. Mismatch causes sync failures or duplicate objects.
solution: PowerShell conversion: ImmutableID→GUID: ="zUEtuG8MDUKSi6xRtkYKrw=="; [guid]=[System.Convert]::FromBase64String(); GUID→ImmutableID: =[guid]::New("51699263-aa4f-47b6-867d-3aa8a9d59fdc"); =[System.Convert]::ToBase64String(.ToByteArray())
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Sync/AAD Connect/Tools/PowerShell to convert between immutableID and GUID.md

symptom: Connectivity issues between AAD Connect server and domain controller or proxy server; need to capture network trace for diagnosis
rootCause: Network-level issues (firewall, routing, TLS handshake failures) between AAD Connect and dependent servers require packet-level analysis.
solution: Capture network trace simultaneously on BOTH source and destination servers. For AD↔AADC issues: capture on domain controller AND AADC server. For AADC↔proxy issues: capture on AADC server AND proxy server. Simultaneous capture is critical for correlating packets.

symptom: Need reference: Sample working SSO trace for Outlook client on AADJ device using WAM/PRT - shows complete token acquisition flow from GetTokenSilently through PRT renewal to cache write
solution: AAD Analytic log (Event ID 1099) flow: 1) GetTokenSilently started (0x4AA5001A) 2) Token request params logged (client=d3590ed6, resource=partner.outlook.cn, authority=login.partner.microsoftonline.cn) 3) Load client from cache using webaccount (0x4AA5011A) 4) Primary user context loaded (0x4AA50017) 5) Read RT from cache file (0x4AA50010, BrokerPlugin LocalState) 6) Send web request to /common/oauth2/token (0x4AA90010) 7) Renew token by PRT success (0x4AA90055) 8) Write renewed RT to cache (0x4AA50013) 9) Operation succeeded (0x4AA5001B)

symptom: Need to decode ITData field from ESTS/EvoSTS Kusto logs to understand token type, auth methods, MFA details, and session timeline
solution: ITData comma-separated. Key indices: [0]=TokenType, [1]=IssuanceTime, [2]=ExpirationTime, [3]=AuthInstant, [4]=AuthMethods, [11]=StrongAuthInstant, [12]=OriginatedRequestId, [18]=BindingData, [21]=FicIssuer, [26]=StrongAuthMethods bitmask, [27]=ResourceTenantMfaContainer, [28]=HomeTenantMfaContainer. AuthMethodModes: 1=Password, 2=Voice, 4=HardwareOath, 8=SoftwareOath, 16=PhoneAppNotification, 32=PhoneAppOTP, 64=Fido, 128=Certificate, 256=FederatedSF, 512=FederatedMF.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Troubleshooting Tools/General Tools/ITData Decoder.md
